title: Add multiline editor to 'rk log add'
description: Launch multiline text editor when no args. Support meeting syntax [meeting:name], task refs [task:id], durations. Preserve 'rk log add "text"' for one-liners.
notes: Implemented in commit 012696b. Replaced logInputModel with logMultilineModel (textarea via components.TextEditor). 10 unit tests added. Pushed to main.

title: Add mini-TUI to 'rk task deadline'
description: Launch fuzzy task picker when no task specified, then date picker. Same pattern as task schedule.

title: Add mini-TUI to 'rk task note'
description: Launch fuzzy task picker + multiline editor when no args. Support: 'rk task note' (mini-TUI) and 'rk task note <id> "Note text"' (CLI).

title: Add mini-TUI to 'rk task schedule'
description: Launch fuzzy task picker when no task specified, then date picker. Support: 'rk task schedule' (mini-TUI) and 'rk task schedule --match "auth" tomorrow' (CLI).

title: Create fuzzy task picker component
description: Build Bubble Tea fuzzy finder for selecting tasks by title. Should show task list, filter as user types, and return selected task ID. Reusable across schedule/deadline/note commands.

title: Add mini-TUI to 'rk task new'
description: Launch form when no args provided: Title, Tags (optional), Schedule (optional), Deadline (optional). Preserve CLI args: 'rk task new "Title" --tags=foo --schedule=tomorrow'.
close_reason: Integrated mini-TUI form into rk task new with full polish and code review approval

title: Create reusable mini-TUI form component
description: Build Bubble Tea component for multi-field forms with tab navigation, validation, and cancellation. Should support text inputs, optional fields, and natural date parsing. Will be used by task/log/note creation commands.
close_reason: Implemented reusable mini-TUI form component with comprehensive tests and documentation

title: [EPIC] Mini-TUI CLI Input System
description: Add mini-TUI prompts for complex CLI inputs to eliminate quoting friction and ID lookup. Includes forms, fuzzy pickers, and multiline editors while preserving scriptable CLI arguments.
close_reason: All tasks completed: schedule, deadline, and note mini-TUIs implemented

title: Add debug-level logging for state changes and operations
description: Add DEBUG-level logs for state transitions, database operations, and file I/O. Include component state updates in TUI. Use RECKON_DEBUG env var to enable. Estimated: 2-3 hours.
close_reason: Implemented comprehensive DEBUG-level logging for state changes, database operations, and file I/O throughout repository, task service, and TUI layers. All logging respects RECKON_DEBUG environment variable.

title: Add slog structured logging to service and repository layers
description: Implement slog-based logging in journal.Service and journal.Repository. Log all errors with context, entry/exit of major operations. Use structured logging with key-value pairs. Estimated: 4-6 hours.

title: Epic: Logging & Instrumentation System
TUI: Fix state mutation bug in submitInput
description:
State reset happens inside the tea.Cmd function instead of in model update, causing potential race conditions.

Location: internal/tui/model.go:290-293



The state changes should happen in the Update() function, not in the command. This violates Bubble Tea's architecture and could cause state corruption.
close_reason: Fixed: State mutation has been properly moved to Update function. Also completed full migration to bubbles/textinput component, removing hybrid state that would have caused input bugs.

title: Subject/body node convention: multi-line bodies, first line displayed
description:
## Want
A durable todo (or any node) should hold a multi-line body while every list surface shows only the first line -- the git-commit shape: line 1 = subject, blank line, rest = body.

## Substrate is already there
todos/<ULID>.md carries an arbitrary markdown body and node.Parse is byte-preserving (internal/node/node.go). Nothing in the node layer needs to change.

## What is missing (above it)
- todo.go:341 addDurableTodo joins argv into a single line; no body path.
- todo.go:535 (todo list) and today.go:305 (rk today) print strings.TrimSpace(body) inline -- a multi-line body smears across the display.
- index has no title: _nodes (index/schema.go:28) stores body only, so every consumer would have to re-split the string.

## Scope
- Document the subject/body convention (first non-empty body line = subject; blank line; remainder = body) in the node spec + AGENTS.md.
- Add a derived title column to _nodes, computed at reconcile, exposed on the nodes view.
- todo list / today / query pretty output render title only. --json keeps the full body plus the new title field.

## Done when
- A todo with a 5-line body lists as one line and round-trips byte-identical.
- rk query can select title without string surgery.

title: Move the one-shot legacy migrator off 'rk import'
description:
## Problem
'rk import' (import.go:20, internal/textmigrate) is the one-shot gen-1 -> vault migrator. It squats the verb the design reserves for write-glue / external ingest (composable-redesign.md: 'pipe + emit-side disposition + generic --import'). T9's plan (D-Name) picked 'import' only because 'rk migrate' was taken by the unrelated pre-pivot xid->slug migrator.

## Scope
Once the v0 cut deletes internal/cli/migrate.go, 'migrate' is free. Move the importer to 'rk migrate legacy' (keeping --dry-run/--verify), leaving 'import' unclaimed for the write-glue seam.

## Done when
- 'rk import' no longer exists; 'rk migrate legacy' does the same work with identical flags and output.
- Idempotency + --verify behavior unchanged (import_test.go retargeted).

title: Retire the v0 DB-primary verb surface
description:
## Problem
root.go:116-136 registers two live command surfaces. The v0 set (log, note new/list, notes, week, rebuild, review, schedule, task, win, checklist, tui) still reads AND WRITES the legacy SQLite DB. Since v1-T9 migrated that data to text-truth, the DB is a stale mirror: 'rk task new', 'rk win add', 'rk tui' write to a store nothing reads back. This is data divergence, not cosmetics.

'note' is the acute case: one cobra parent hosts legacy new/list (note.go:211) and v1 create/show/rename/index (note_v1.go:139).

## Scope
- Delete the v0 commands and the service/repository layers behind them that nothing else uses (journal, service/notes, checklist service, storage journal paths, internal/cli/migrate.go).
- Delete 'rk tui' (stubs.go) -- it writes the stale DB. It returns in the TUI-rebuild ticket.
- KEEP internal/tui/components/ intact: presentation only, reused by the composable-prompt layer.
- Drop initServiceE / the requiresDB annotation machinery once nothing needs it.

## Done when
- 'rk help' shows only vault-native verbs.
- No code path opens the legacy operational DB.
- Tests for deleted surfaces are removed, not skipped; suite green.

title: [EPIC] v1 consolidation: retire v0, composable prompts, TUI rebuild
description: Post-T9 cleanup and the human-facing porcelain. Three phases: (A) delete the v0 DB-primary verb surface now that text is truth, (B) subject/body node convention so durable nodes can be multi-line with a git-commit-style first line, (C) rebuild the interactive layer as composable prompts over the v1 verbs, with the full TUI on top. Design basis: composable-redesign.md:874-878 -- TUI/CLI/MCP are peer porcelains over one verb surface.

title: work-ticket skill: TDD red-state gate can't distinguish 'red' from 'broken'
description:
Found via fable-model critique of .claude/skills/work-ticket/SKILL.md (2026-07-02), verified: go build does not compile _test.go files (confirmed Go toolchain behavior, not repo-specific).

Phase 3 validation (~line 179-180):
  go build ./...   # Must succeed (compiles)
  go test ./...    # Must fail (red state — feature not implemented)

'go build' never compiles _test.go files, so 'must succeed (compiles)' validates nothing about the tests just written -- a test file with a syntax error, bad import, or undefined symbol passes this gate silently. Then 'go test ./... must fail' is the ONLY red-state signal, and a nonzero exit code means the same thing whether the new tests correctly fail (desired), the test file doesn't compile, a pre-existing test flaked, or an unrelated package broke -- all four look identical to the gate.

Same shallow signal reused in Phase 4 (~line 227): 'Success criterion: go test ./... exits 0' -- an implementer could satisfy this by deleting the failing assertion, or the pipeline could treat a compile error as legitimate red state and 'implement' against tests that were never syntactically valid.

Fix (per critique): run 'go test -run "^$" ./...' first -- executes zero tests but forces compilation, catching bad test files immediately and separately from behavioral red state. Then run only the newly-written tests by name and require actual '--- FAIL: TestX' lines naming them, not just a nonzero exit code.

Acceptance criteria:
- Phase 3's gate fails distinctly and legibly when a test file doesn't compile, vs when it compiles but correctly fails.
- Phase 4's success criterion requires the previously-failing named tests to now pass, not just a bare exit-0 across the whole module.

title: work-ticket skill: worktree branches from stale HEAD + claims ticket before worktree creation succeeds
description:
Found via fable-model critique of .claude/skills/work-ticket/SKILL.md (2026-07-02), verified against the repo (7 stale .worktrees/* dirs currently present: reckon-4u1c, gcuu, iqv5, obed, sa8l, w6qh-rename-note, zw72-notes-edit -- consistent with this exact failure mode).

Phase 0 (~line 62-79):
1. 'bd update <ticket-id> --status=in_progress' runs BEFORE 'git worktree add' -- if worktree creation fails (e.g. a stale branch/dir left from a prior aborted run), the ticket is left claimed/in_progress with no worktree and no cleanup path. Escalation paths elsewhere in the skill never reset ticket status.
2. 'git worktree add .worktrees/<ticket-id> -b <ticket-id>' has no preceding fetch and does not specify a base ref -- it branches from whatever the main checkout's HEAD happens to be, not origin/main. Can silently start work on a stale base.

Note: the simpler global command at ~/.claude/commands/work-ticket.md gets this right (fetch, then branch off origin/main explicitly, claim via bd update --claim).

Acceptance criteria:
- Fetch + explicit origin/main base before worktree creation.
- Claim (bd update --status=in_progress) happens AFTER worktree creation succeeds, or a failure path un-claims on error.
- Stale .worktrees/* dirs from prior runs are handled (detected/reused/cleaned), not silently left behind.
- Existing 7 stale .worktrees/* dirs cleaned up as part of or alongside this fix (verify none hold unmerged work first).

title: work-ticket skill: Plan subagent has no Write tool but Phase 2 requires it to output plan.md
description:
Found via fable-model critique of .claude/skills/work-ticket/SKILL.md (2026-07-02), verified against this session's own agent-type tool list.

Phase 2 (~line 118-137) invokes Task tool with subagent_type="Plan" and requires 'Output: .claude/work/<ticket-id>/plan.md', then validates the file exists. The Plan subagent type's tool list is 'All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit' -- it CANNOT write a file. Phase 2 fails structurally on attempt 1 every single run, burning one of the 2 max retries for a failure mode that retrying can never fix.

Fix: the orchestrator (the agent running the skill, not the Plan subagent) must write the Plan subagent's returned text to disk at the target path, OR Phase 2 must invoke a subagent_type with Write access (e.g. general-purpose) instead of Plan.

Acceptance criteria:
- Phase 2 produces plan.md on a clean first attempt without relying on a subagent that lacks Write.
- The 2-attempt retry budget in Phase 2 is preserved for actual plan-quality failures, not tooling failures.

title: work-ticket skill: artifact path broken (.claude/ gitignored + worktree/main-repo path mismatch)
description:
Found via fable-model critique of .claude/skills/work-ticket/SKILL.md (2026-07-02), verified against the repo.

Two compounding bugs in the same root cause:
1. .claude/ is in .gitignore (line 21). Phase 2's commit step (SKILL.md ~line 149: 'git add .claude/work/<ticket-id>/') will fail every time with 'paths ignored by .gitignore'.
2. Phase 0 (~line 73-74) says the work dir is created 'in main repo, not worktree', but Phase 2 (~line 148) does 'cd .worktrees/<ticket-id>' THEN tries to add that same relative path -- which does not exist inside the worktree's checkout at all. Path mismatch, not just gitignore.
3. This also poisons Phase 7's pattern-frequency grep (~line 316: 'grep -irl "$pattern" .claude/work/*/review.md') -- reading from a directory that was never successfully committed/shared.

DIRECTIVE (owner, 2026-07-02): relocate ticket-work artifacts (plan.md, codebase-analysis.md, acceptance-criteria.md, preflight-report.md, review.md, summary.md, pattern-frequency.txt, state.json) to a location OUTSIDE .claude/ entirely.

Recommended default (not mandatory -- implementer's call): a plain, non-dot, git-tracked directory INSIDE the worktree itself, e.g. ticket-work/<ticket-id>/ -- this simultaneously fixes the Phase 0/Phase 2 path-mismatch bug (one consistent path, no main-repo-vs-worktree split needed) and makes the plan/analysis/review travel with the ticket's own branch/PR history (useful reviewer context), to be squashed/cleaned at merge if desired.

Acceptance criteria:
- Every phase (0,2,3,5,6,7,8) and state.json reference ONE consistent, non-.claude, git-committable path.
- Phase 2's git add/commit actually succeeds (test it).
- Phase 7's pattern-frequency grep reads from the same path artifacts are actually written to.
- docs/agents/*.md references to .claude/work/ updated to match.

title: Review H1: shared Render(node) + create-round-trip gate
description: From foundation-review-2026-06-24 (H1, gating). The byte-preserving keystone covered EDIT but not CREATE; node.Mint was unused and there was no shared model->text primitive, so each create/import path would re-solve markdown rendering (where old writer.go got lossy) and the create path was ungated. Built node.Render(n) (typed fields -> canonical markdown, inverse of deriveView+extractBody) + node.NewNode (mint-on-create), with a gate test asserting parse(render(n)) reproduces the node, serialize(parse(render(n)))==render(n), render idempotence, and Obsidian-shaped quoted refs. internal/node/render.go + render_test.go.
close_reason: DONE this session. node.Render + node.NewNode + create-round-trip gate (render_test.go) all pass; full node/index/output/config suites green; build OK. Create path now held to the same round-trip discipline as the edit path.

title: Review M5: resolve legacy ~/.reckon config overlap before tools write the vault
description: From foundation-review-2026-06-24 (M5). Legacy config funcs (DataDir/JournalDir/TasksDir/NotesDir/DatabasePath/LogDir) default to ~/.reckon AND the new Config.VaultDir also defaults to ~/.reckon — two truth models (old DB-primary reckon.db+journal/*.md vs new text vault) sharing one directory = collision/migration hazard. Legacy funcs also mkdir as a side effect, contradicting the new resolution-is-pure principle. Done when: the new vault default is separated from the legacy app-data dir (decide the v1 vault default), legacy and new config surfaces no longer collide, and the new path stays pure (no mkdir on resolve). Must land before T4 writes into the vault. Ties to T9 migration.

title: Review M3/M4: duplicate-ULID + alias-collision detection in reconcile
description: From foundation-review-2026-06-24 (M3,M4). _nodes.ulid is not UNIQUE and _aliases allows one alias on two nodes, so a copied/Syncthing-duplicated file or a colliding Obsidian alias resolves arbitrarily and SILENTLY. The design says the index enforces uniqueness. Cheap now, ugly once data exists. Done when: reconcile detects two node_keys sharing a non-empty ULID and warns (lists the files); detects an alias mapped to >1 node_key and warns; both surfaced in rk index output and covered by tests. Decide enforce-vs-warn (warn is the floor).

title: v1-T4: log tool + capture (rk log) end-to-end
description: The highest-use surface: append-only daily log, replacing Logseq journal+recall. rk log/add appends a log-entry node to the day group file via span-local write-back (no full-file regen); log-day group parser yields N+1 nodes (day + entries); author/provenance stamped; AT= backfill for time. Relies on merge=union (T0) for lossless concurrent appends. Done when: rk log appends and the file round-trips; the day parses to day+entry nodes; the entry is indexed and retrievable via rk query (capture->index->query proven end-to-end); provenance present on every entry.

title: v1-T3: rk query (read surface, SQL over stable views, read-only)
description: The agent+human retrieval surface. SQL executed against the T2 stable views; READ-ONLY enforced (query_only/mode=ro, reject non-SELECT); output = canonical node NDJSON by default plus a raw-rows mode for aggregates; named versioned saved views (rk query --view NAME); --lang seam defaulting to sql; agent-ergonomic (projection/--fields/--limit for token economy). Done when: queries over the indexed corpus return correct nodes; a non-SELECT is rejected; NDJSON output composes through a pipe; a saved view runs; read-only is enforced under test.

title: v1-T2: SQLite property-graph index (schema + indexer + lazy reconcile)
description: The derived, disposable, per-device index. Tables nodes/edges/fts/aliases behind STABLE PUBLIC VIEWS (physical tables private); per-file meta (path,ulid-set,hash,mtime) as the change-detection authority + global index_meta (schema_version,last_reconcile_at,last_full_rebuild_at,vault_id,builder_version); file->node->upsert via the T1 parser; lazy reconcile-on-read (hash-authoritative, mtime fast-path); rk index full rebuild; WAL + single reconcile-writer lock; lives in the cache dir, never synced. Done when: indexing a corpus dir populates the views; rk index rebuild is deterministic; reconcile picks up add/edit/delete/rename (rename keyed by ULID, loc updated); a schema_version bump auto-rebuilds; all tested.
close_reason: Merged to main as PR #139 (commit 37928d1); was left in_progress. v1-T2 SQLite property-graph index is shipped.

title: v1-T1: canonical node package (productionize the round-trip spike)
description: Promote internal/spike/roundtrip into the real internal/node package. Canonical node model with fields ulid,type,time,author,body,aliases,props,fragments,links,loc; byte-preserving parse + serialize + span-local field edit (proven by the spike); NDJSON envelope marshal/unmarshal; ULID minting; a per-tool parser interface (parse/serialize pair). Done when: round-trip identity + fuzz gate pass on the corpus (carried from the spike, 0 failures); envelope marshal/unmarshal round-trips; span-local SetField is surgical; ULIDs are stable and time-sortable. Builds on reckon-k9ff (spike PASSED).

title: v1-T0: rk dispatcher + config + vault/index layout + output conventions
description: Foundation the v1 tools hang off. Multi-call rk binary (Cobra, git-style dispatch) with the rk-<name> PATH extension point; config resolves the vault dir and a per-device index cache dir (never synced, gitignored); scaffold .gitattributes with merge=union for log/*.md (watch-item #3, log-only); a shared agent-ergonomic output helper (the bd standard: --json/NDJSON for agents beside a human-pretty mode). Done when: rk --help lists stub subcommands; config resolves vault+cache paths in a temp vault (unit-tested); output helper emits json/ndjson/pretty and is unit-tested; gitattributes scoped to log/*.md only.

title: Spike: parse/serialize round-trip + span-local write-back (gating)
description: GATING Group-A spike from the composable redesign (watch-item #1). Prove span-local write-back: a tool can rewrite ONLY the spans it owns while byte-preserving all unmodeled prose, and parse(serialize(parse(f)))==f holds on real Obsidian/Logseq/agent input. Per the round-2 assessment, pre-name a fallback if the bet fails (e.g. DB-truth for the single most-structured type) so a failed spike reroutes the design instead of stranding it. Self-contained package internal/spike/roundtrip; not entangled with the brittle journal parser. Output: working code + adversarial corpus + property tests + a written verdict.
close_reason: PASS — span-local write-back proven. internal/spike/roundtrip/: serialize(parse(f))==f byte-identical across corpus, surgical span edits, group-file sibling preservation, conflict-marker refusal, ~396k-exec fuzz with 0 failures. Verdict + pre-named fallback (per-type DB-truth retreat) in docs/design/spike-roundtrip-verdict.md. Keystone de-risked; proceed to v1 sequence.

title: Add description field to task creation TUI form
description:
The inline TUI form for task creation (launched by 'rk task new' with no args) has fields for Title, Tags, Schedule, and Deadline — but no Description. The task file format already has a '## Description' section, but it just echoes the title text. There is no real description field on the Task model or in the service layer.

## Problem
- Tasks have no way to capture a description/body beyond the title
- The '## Description' section in the markdown file just duplicates the title (task_writer.go:51)
- The parser doesn't read a description back from the file
- The Task struct has no Description field

## What needs to be done

### Model: add Description field to Task
- Add Description string field to the Task struct in models.go
- Update NewTask to accept or default description

### Storage: wire description through read/write
- Update task_writer.go to write the actual description (not title) into '## Description'
- Update task_parser.go to parse the '## Description' section into the Description field
- Update task_service.go AddTask to accept a description parameter
- Update database storage if tasks are also stored in SQLite

### TUI form: add Description field
- Add a Description field to the task creation form in launchTaskNewForm()
- Use the TextEditor component (same as log.go) or a multiline form field type
- Description should be optional — title-only tasks are still fine
- Pass description through to createTaskFromForm → AddTask

### CLI flag
- Add --description flag to 'rk task new' for non-interactive use

## Design notes
- The form field order should be: Title, Description, Tags, Schedule, Deadline
- Description can be a simple multiline text field or a single-line field (match what the Form component supports)
- Backward compatible: existing tasks without descriptions should parse fine (empty description)
close_reason: Cleanup 2026-06-24: stale in_progress / decomposed. 53fu decomposed into v1-T* + review tickets (see docs/design/composable-redesign.md). 4u1c#136, elfb#133, obed#134, pjjr#132, gcuu#135 merged to main.

title: Interactive TUI for checklist runs
description:
The checklist CLI commands are functional but high-friction for human use. Checking off items requires typing 'rk cl check <pos> --template=<name>' repeatedly. There is also no way to abandon a checklist run without immediately starting a new one (reset = abandon + start).

## Problem
- Checking items requires long CLI commands with --template flag each time
- No way to simply abandon a run (reset always starts a new one)
- Browsing active runs and toggling items should be interactive, not command-driven
- The current CLI is optimized for LLM agents, not humans at a terminal

## What needs to be done

### Inline TUI for checklist runs
Build a compact, inline Bubbletea program (NOT full-screen/alt-screen) for working with checklist runs. Follow the pattern used by existing inline TUIs like the task picker (task_picker_helper.go) and log multiline editor (log.go) — these use tea.NewProgram() without WithAltScreen().

**Launch via 'rk cl run <template>'** — this is the primary entry point. If the template has an active run, resume it. If not, start a new run and enter the TUI.

- Show checklist items with checkboxes inline in the terminal
- Arrow keys (j/k) to navigate items, space/enter to toggle check
- Auto-complete detection: when all items checked, show completion message and exit
- 'a' to abandon the current run (mark abandoned, exit — does NOT start a new one)
- 'q' / Esc to quit the TUI while keeping the run active for later
- Compact rendering: just the checklist name, items with checkboxes, and a hint line for keybindings

### Service layer: add AbandonRun method
- Add AbandonRun(nameOrID) to checklist.Service that marks a run as abandoned WITHOUT starting a new one
- ResetRun should continue to work as-is (abandon + start fresh)
- This gives both TUI and CLI a clean abandon path

### CLI shortcut
- Add 'rk cl abandon <template>' as a CLI command for non-TUI abandon

## Design notes
- Inline TUI, not full-screen — same pattern as task_picker_helper.go and log.go
- The TUI is a standalone Bubbletea model in internal/cli/ (not internal/tui/) since it follows the same pattern as other CLI inline TUIs
- Keep it simple: one screen, one checklist at a time
- 'rk cl start' remains for non-interactive use (LLM agents); 'rk cl run' is the human-friendly entry point

title: Simplify TUI task list: replace list.Model with []Task + selectedIndex
description:
## Problem

The TUI task list has a ghost-selection bug: completed tasks can be selected even though they're not displayed. This is caused by a fundamental architecture issue — the `TaskList` component wraps Bubble Tea's `list.Model` for cursor tracking, but the rendering path (`renderTasksWithDetailPane`) never calls `TaskList.View()`. Instead it gets tasks via `GetTasks()`, groups them through `GroupTasksByTime()`, and renders via `renderTaskSection()`. The cursor and the display are two parallel views of the same data that get out of sync.

This bug has been worked on multiple times without being fixed because the root cause is architectural, not a missing filter.

## Solution

Replace the `TaskList` component entirely with two plain fields on `Model`:
- `tasks []journal.Task` — open tasks sorted by priority
- `selectedIndex int` — cursor position into the displayed list

Single source of truth. No hidden cursor state. No sync issues.

## Design Decisions

**Priority sort replaces time-grouped sections.** Instead of TODAY / THIS WEEK / ALL TASKS sections with boundary-crossing navigation, a single sorted list: overdue → due today/scheduled today → due this week/scheduled this week → everything else by creation date. Same urgency signal, zero section machinery.

**Notes move to a detail area.** Instead of interleaving note items in the cursor list (which required skip-note navigation, collapse state, `IsSelectedItemNote`), show notes for the selected task in a fixed area below the list.

**Keep:** add task, edit task, toggle done (archive), add note, tags, schedule/deadline dates, date picker modal, date urgency indicators.

**Remove:** `TaskList` component, `TimeGroupedTaskList`, `list.Model`/`TaskItem`/`TaskDelegate`/`buildTaskItems`, collapsible notes in task list, clear date submenu (`c`), delete task from TUI (`d`), detail pane positioning, notes pane stub, `SectionNotes`.

## Implementation Steps

Each step leaves `go build` and `go test` passing.

### Step 1 — Add `SortTasksByPriority` with unit tests
Add to `model.go`. Bucket 0: overdue deadline. Bucket 1: due today or scheduled today/past. Bucket 2: due/scheduled this week. Bucket 3: everything else. Filter out done tasks. Stable sort by CreatedAt within buckets. Table-driven tests.

### Step 2 — Add `tasks`/`selectedIndex` to Model alongside `taskList`
Populate in `handleTasksLoaded` after existing `taskList.UpdateTasks`. Both paths coexist temporarily.

### Step 3 — Implement new render methods (unused)
Add `renderTaskList(width, height)` — simple loop over `m.tasks`, highlight selected, scroll offset. Add `renderDetailArea(width, height)` — shows notes for selected task. Dead code until Step 4.

### Step 4 — Swap rendering, remove section layout infrastructure
Replace `renderTasksWithDetailPane` call in `renderNewLayout` with new methods. Delete: `renderTasksWithDetailPane`, `renderDetailPane`, `renderTaskSection`, `DetailPanePosition`, `getTaskSection`, `calculateDetailPanePosition`, `TaskSectionDimensions`, `CalculateTaskSectionDimensions`, `notesPaneVisible`, notes pane from model. Delete `model_selection_test.go`.

### Step 5 — Wire keyboard navigation to `selectedIndex`
j/k = `clampIndex(selectedIndex±1)`. Space reads `m.tasks[m.selectedIndex]`. Update `handleAddNote`, `handleEditTask`, `handleScheduleTask`, `handleSetDeadline`. Remove: `handleDeleteTask`, `handleClearDate`, `handleClearDateKeys`, `clearDateMode`, `c`/`d` keybindings for tasks, enter-to-expand for tasks, `TaskSelectionChangedMsg`, `handleTaskSelectionChanged`, `updateNotesForSelectedTask`, `updateLinksForSelectedItem`, `selectedTaskID`.

### Step 6 — Delete `TaskList` component and `list.Model` infrastructure
Delete from `task_list.go`: `TaskList`, `TimeGroupedTaskList`, `TaskItem`, `TaskDelegate`, `buildTaskItems`, `GroupTasksByTime`, etc. Keep: `parseDate`, `formatDateInfo`, `formatFriendlyDate`, `getDateStyle`, style vars, `TaskToggleMsg`. Remove `taskList` field from Model. Delete `task_list_example_test.go`. Rewrite `task_list_test.go` for surviving helpers.

### Step 7 — Remove `SectionNotes` and notes pane plumbing
`SectionCount` → 2. Remove `N` keybinding, `handleToggleNotesPane`, `handleLinkSelected`, `handleLinksLoaded`, `linksLoadedMsg`, `loadLinksForNote`. Update help view.

### Step 8 — Rewrite model tests
Tests for: `SortTasksByPriority`, `renderTaskList` empty/selection/scroll, `renderDetailArea` empty/with-notes, help view updated shortcuts.

### Step 9 — Final cleanup
Remove collapse indicator constants if unreferenced. Dead-code grep sweep. `go vet`.

## Risks

- `formatDateInfo`/`getDateStyle` may not be exported — export in Step 3
- Step 4 is the largest change — can split into 4a (swap render) and 4b (delete old code)
- `TaskNoteDeleteMsg` exists in both `model.go` and `components` package — remove both in Step 6
- Scroll offset logic needs explicit test coverage (Step 8)

## Out of Scope

- Task note deletion from detail area (follow-on)
- Scroll indicator / scroll bar
- Task filtering or search
- Notes/wiki pane (separate initiative)

title: Bug: task cursor navigates through hidden tasks
description: In the TUI task list, pressing j/k moves the cursor through tasks that are not visible, causing jarring jumps where the visible selection appears to skip. The TaskList uses Bubble Tea's list.Model (internal/tui/components/task_list.go lines 230-237) with GroupTasksByTime() grouping (lines 544-586). Tasks are rendered as separate sections with detail pane (renderTasksWithDetailPane in model.go lines 661-740) but the underlying list model likely still navigates the full unfiltered set. Fix: cursor navigation should only visit tasks that are currently rendered/visible.

title: Consolidate TUI to logs and tasks panes only

Current layout: 40% logs / 40% tasks / 18% right sidebar (schedule 30% + intentions 35% + wins 35%). New layout: two panes, logs (left ~50%) and tasks (right ~50%). Remove ScheduleView, IntentionList, WinsView components from TUI model and rendering. Remove SectionSchedule, SectionIntentions, SectionWins from Section enum and focus cycling. Update CalculatePaneDimensions in layout.go. The CLI commands for schedule/win remain; only the TUI display is removed.

title: Bug: TUI clips top line and has spurious line below text entry
description: Two related layout issues in the TUI: (1) the top line of content is cut off/clipped, and (2) there is an extra blank line appearing below the text entry bar. Both are likely off-by-one errors in the lipgloss layout height calculations. Key files: internal/tui/layout.go CalculatePaneDimensions (lines 36-104), internal/tui/model.go renderNewLayout (lines 743-880). The bottom bars (TextEntry + StatusBar) are joined after the main pane layout - check height accounting there.
notes:
Root cause identified. In renderNewLayout() (model.go:870):

    mainView := content + "\n" + textEntry + "\n" + successMsg + "\n" + summary + "\n" + status

When successMsg is empty string (the common case), the join produces:
    content + textEntry + "" + summary + status with 4 explicit newlines

Height breakdown for a 50-line terminal:
- availableHeight = 50 - 5 = 45 (correctly subtracts TextEntryHeight(3) + SummaryHeight(1) + StatusHeight(1))
- content: 45 lines
- textEntry: 3 lines
- empty successMsg: still counts as 1 blank line due to surrounding "\n"s
- summary: 1 line
- status: 1 line
Total: 51 lines rendered into a 50-line terminal → top line clipped

The 'spurious line below text entry' is the blank line created by "\n" + "" + "\n" when successMsg is empty.

Fix: conditionally include successMsg/summary in the join only when non-empty:
    parts := []string{content, textEntry}
    if successMsg != "" { parts = append(parts, successMsg) }
    if summary != "" { parts = append(parts, summary) }
    parts = append(parts, status)
    mainView = strings.Join(parts, "\n")

Confirmed via tmux capture-pane + ansi2html + playwright screenshot: top border (┌──┐) of all three panes is clipped off screen.
close_reason: Fixed: conditional slice join in renderNewLayout() eliminates phantom blank lines. Verified visually via tmux+playwright.

title: Fix logging interference with TUI display
description:
## Problem
The new logging system interferes with the TUI display by writing to stdout/stderr, causing visual corruption of the Bubbletea interface.

## Solution Approach
Redirect logging output away from stdout/stderr to avoid conflicts with the TUI. Common approaches:

### Option 1: File-based logging (Recommended)
- Create a log file in a standard location (e.g., `~/.reckon/logs/reckon.log` or `/tmp/reckon-{pid}.log`)
- Use log rotation to prevent unbounded growth (e.g., keep last 5 files, max 10MB each)
- Consider structured logging (JSON) for easier parsing/debugging
- Add a `--log-file` flag to allow users to specify custom location
- Add a `--debug` or `--log-level` flag to control verbosity

### Option 2: Conditional logging
- Only log to stderr when NOT in TUI mode
- Detect TUI mode and redirect logs appropriately
- Use a logger that can switch outputs dynamically

### Option 3: Separate debug output channel
- Use Go's standard `log` package with custom writer
- Implement `io.Writer` that routes to file instead of stderr
- Ensure all logging calls use this custom writer

## Implementation Details

1. **Update logging initialization** (likely in main.go or a logging package):
   - Detect if running in TUI mode
   - Set up file-based logger with rotation
   - Configure log level from environment or flags

2. **Common locations to update**:
   - `cmd/` - CLI entry points
   - Anywhere using `fmt.Printf`, `log.Printf`, `fmt.Println` for debug output
   - Any libraries that log directly to stdout/stderr

3. **Testing**:
   - Verify TUI displays correctly without corruption
   - Verify logs are written to file
   - Test log rotation if implemented
   - Ensure non-TUI commands can still log to stderr if desired

4. **Consider using existing Go logging libraries**:
   - `github.com/rs/zerolog` - zero allocation structured logging
   - `github.com/sirupsen/logrus` - popular structured logger
   - `go.uber.org/zap` - fast structured logging
   - `gopkg.in/natefinch/lumberjack.v2` - log rotation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TUI displays without any logging interference
- [ ] Logs are written to a file location
- [ ] Log location is documented or shown to user
- [ ] Log level can be controlled (at least via flag or env var)
- [ ] Existing debug information is preserved in logs

title: TUI and CLI use separate task systems - tasks don't sync between interfaces
description:
## Problem
Tasks added in TUI don't appear in CLI listings (`rk task list`), and tasks added in CLI don't appear in TUI task list. This creates a fractured user experience where the same application has two separate task databases.

## Root Cause
- TUI uses journal system: stores in `tasks.md` file and `tasks`/`task_notes` DB tables
- CLI uses legacy system: stores in `phase2_tasks`/`phase2_task_tags`/`phase2_task_log_entries` DB tables + individual `.md` files  
- No data synchronization between systems

## Evidence
- TUI loads journal tasks at startup, legacy tasks only on-demand
- CLI task commands only use `task.Service`, ignore journal tasks
- Only log entries are dual-written; task creation/deletion not synced

## Proposed Solution
Unify task systems by migrating everything to the journal system (newer, simpler) as single source of truth. Update CLI to use journal.TaskService instead of legacy task.Service.

## Files Involved
- `internal/tui/model.go` (loads both but separately)
- `internal/cli/task.go` (only uses legacy system)
- `internal/journal/task_service.go` vs `internal/task/service.go`

title: SQL injection vulnerability in FindStaleTasks using fmt.Sprintf
description: In internal/task/repository.go:189, FindStaleTasks uses fmt.Sprintf(query, days) to inject the days parameter into SQL. While days is an int (low risk), this is a dangerous pattern. Fix: Use parameterized query with date('now', '-' || ? || ' days') syntax.
close_reason: Fixed SQL injection vulnerability by replacing fmt.Sprintf with parameterized query. Code reviewed and approved. PR #45 merged.
