title: Implement wiki-style link detection and parsing
description:
## Overview
Implement robust parsing and detection of wiki-style links (`[[note-slug]]` and `[[note-slug|display text]]`) in note content. This is the core linking mechanism for the zettelkasten notes system, enabling bidirectional links and knowledge graph navigation.

## Background Context
- Wiki-style links are the primary way notes reference each other
- Two syntaxes: `[[note-slug]]` for simple links and `[[note-slug|display text]]` for custom link text
- Links must be detected in note content, stored in the `note_links` table, and kept in sync when notes are edited
- Must handle edge cases like malformed links, non-existent target notes, and special characters
- Schema already defined in reckon-63n (notes and note_links tables)

## Acceptance Criteria

### 1. Link Syntax Support
Support two wiki-link formats:
- `[[note-slug]]` - Simple link, displays as "note-slug"
- `[[note-slug|Display Text]]` - Custom display text

**Examples**:
```markdown
See [[zettelkasten-method]] for more details.
Read about [[atomic-notes|the concept of atomic notes]].
Links can be [[inline]] or at the end of sentences.
```

- [ ] Detect both link syntaxes in markdown content
- [ ] Handle multiple links in same line
- [ ] Handle links across multiple lines (edge case)
- [ ] Ignore escaped links: `\[[not-a-link]]`
- [ ] Ignore links in code blocks and inline code

### 2. Link Parsing & Extraction

Create link parser in `internal/parser/links.go`:

```go
package parser

import "regexp"

// Link represents a wiki-style link found in content
type Link struct {
    Raw         string  // Full match: [[slug|text]]
    TargetSlug  string  // The note being linked to
    DisplayText string  // Custom text, or empty if not specified
    Position    int     // Character position in content
}

// ExtractWikiLinks finds all wiki-style links in markdown content
func ExtractWikiLinks(content string) []Link {
    // Regex: \[\[([^|\]]+)(?:\|([^\]]+))?\]\]
    // Group 1: note-slug
    // Group 2: display text (optional)
}

// ValidateSlug checks if a slug is valid for linking
func ValidateSlug(slug string) error {
    // Must be lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens
    // Max length 100 chars
    // No leading/trailing hyphens
}
```

**Regex Pattern**:
```
\[\[([^|\]]+)(?:\|([^\]]+))?\]\]
```
- `\[\[` - Opening brackets
- `([^|\]]+)` - Capture group 1: slug (anything except | or ])
- `(?:\|([^\]]+))?` - Optional: pipe and capture group 2 (display text)
- `\]\]` - Closing brackets

- [ ] Implement `ExtractWikiLinks` function
- [ ] Trim whitespace from slug and display text
- [ ] Return links in order of appearance
- [ ] Handle edge case: Empty slug `[[]]` (invalid)
- [ ] Handle edge case: Slug with spaces (normalize to hyphens)

### 3. Link Storage in Database

When a note is created or updated:
1. Extract all wiki links from content
2. For each link:
   - Create `NoteLink` record with `source_note_id` and `target_slug`
   - Look up target note by slug to set `target_note_id` (can be NULL if not exists)
   - Store `link_type = 'wiki'`
3. Delete old links for this note that are no longer in content

**Service Layer** (`internal/service/note_service.go`):

```go
// UpdateNoteLinks extracts links and syncs to database
func (s *NoteService) UpdateNoteLinks(noteID uint, content string) error {
    links := parser.ExtractWikiLinks(content)
    
    // Start transaction
    tx := s.db.Begin()
    defer tx.Rollback()
    
    // Delete existing links for this note
    tx.Where("source_note_id = ?", noteID).Delete(&models.NoteLink{})
    
    // Insert new links
    for _, link := range links {
        // Look up target note
        var targetNote models.Note
        targetExists := tx.Where("slug = ?", link.TargetSlug).First(&targetNote).Error == nil
        
        noteLink := &models.NoteLink{
            SourceNoteID: noteID,
            TargetSlug:   link.TargetSlug,
            LinkType:     "wiki",
        }
        
        if targetExists {
            noteLink.TargetNoteID = &targetNote.ID
        }
        
        if err := tx.Create(noteLink).Error; err != nil {
            return err
        }
    }
    
    return tx.Commit().Error
}
```

- [ ] Implement `UpdateNoteLinks` in NoteService
- [ ] Delete old links in transaction before inserting new
- [ ] Handle target_note_id being NULL for non-existent notes
- [ ] Prevent duplicate links (schema has UNIQUE constraint)

### 4. Backlink Resolution

When a new note is created, check if any existing links reference it:

```go
// ResolveOrphanedBacklinks finds links to this slug and updates target_note_id
func (s *NoteService) ResolveOrphanedBacklinks(slug string, noteID uint) error {
    return s.db.Model(&models.NoteLink{}).
        Where("target_slug = ? AND target_note_id IS NULL", slug).
        Update("target_note_id", noteID).Error
}
```

Call this after creating a new note to "connect" any previously orphaned links.

- [ ] Implement `ResolveOrphanedBacklinks`
- [ ] Call automatically when new note created
- [ ] Handle case where multiple notes reference same slug

### 5. Link Validation

**Valid slugs**:
- Lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens
- No spaces (spaces in link text converted to hyphens)
- Max 100 characters
- No leading/trailing hyphens

**Invalid links to handle**:
- `[[]]` - Empty slug (skip)
- `[[note with spaces]]` - Normalize to `note-with-spaces`
- `[[UPPERCASE]]` - Normalize to `uppercase`
- `[[slug-with-émojis🎉]]` - Remove or handle special chars

- [ ] Normalize slugs during parsing (lowercase, spaces to hyphens)
- [ ] Skip invalid/empty links
- [ ] Log warnings for malformed links (don't fail)

### 6. Code Block Exclusion

Don't parse links inside code blocks:

```markdown
This is a [[real-link]].

```
This is [[not-a-link]] in a code block.
```

Also `[[not-a-link]]` in inline code.
```

**Implementation**:
```go
func ExtractWikiLinks(content string) []Link {
    // First, identify code block ranges
    codeBlocks := findCodeBlocks(content)
    inlineCode := findInlineCode(content)
    
    // Extract all link matches
    matches := wikiLinkRegex.FindAllStringSubmatchIndex(content, -1)
    
    // Filter out links inside code blocks
    var links []Link
    for _, match := range matches {
        pos := match[0]
        if !isInCodeBlock(pos, codeBlocks, inlineCode) {
            // Extract link
        }
    }
    return links
}
```

- [ ] Detect fenced code blocks (```) and skip links within
- [ ] Detect inline code (`text`) and skip links within
- [ ] Handle edge case: Code blocks with different fence styles (~~~, ```)

### 7. Integration with Note Parser

Update note parser to call link extraction:

**File**: `internal/parser/note.go`

```go
func ParseNote(filePath string) (*models.Note, error) {
    content, frontmatter, err := parseMarkdown(filePath)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    note := &models.Note{
        Title:    frontmatter.Title,
        Slug:     frontmatter.Slug,
        FilePath: filePath,
        // ... other fields
    }
    
    // Note: Link extraction happens in service layer after note saved
    // because we need note.ID to create NoteLink records
    
    return note, nil
}
```

- [ ] Extract links in service layer, not parser
- [ ] Call `UpdateNoteLinks` after note created/updated
- [ ] Ensure transactional consistency

## Implementation Guidance

### File Structure
```
internal/
  parser/
    links.go          # NEW: Wiki link parsing logic
    links_test.go     # NEW: Link parsing tests
  service/
    note_service.go   # MODIFY: Add UpdateNoteLinks, ResolveOrphanedBacklinks
```

### Regex Tips
- Use `regexp.MustCompile` at package level for performance
- Use `FindAllStringSubmatchIndex` to get positions
- Test regex thoroughly with edge cases

### Performance Considerations
- Compile regex once at package initialization
- Use transactions for bulk link updates
- Index `target_slug` column for fast backlink queries (already in schema)

### Testing Requirements

Create `internal/parser/links_test.go`:

**Test cases**:
```go
func TestExtractWikiLinks(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name     string
        content  string
        expected []Link
    }{
        {
            name:    "simple link",
            content: "See [[note-slug]] for details",
            expected: []Link{{TargetSlug: "note-slug"}},
        },
        {
            name:    "link with display text",
            content: "See [[note-slug|custom text]] here",
            expected: []Link{{TargetSlug: "note-slug", DisplayText: "custom text"}},
        },
        {
            name:    "multiple links",
            content: "See [[note-1]] and [[note-2|second note]]",
            expected: []Link{
                {TargetSlug: "note-1"},
                {TargetSlug: "note-2", DisplayText: "second note"},
            },
        },
        {
            name:    "link in code block",
            content: "Normal [[link]]\n```\nCode [[not-link]]\n```",
            expected: []Link{{TargetSlug: "link"}},
        },
        {
            name:    "link in inline code",
            content: "Normal [[link]] and `code [[not-link]]`",
            expected: []Link{{TargetSlug: "link"}},
        },
        {
            name:    "escaped link",
            content: "Escaped \\[[not-link]] vs [[real-link]]",
            expected: []Link{{TargetSlug: "real-link"}},
        },
        {
            name:    "empty link",
            content: "Empty [[]] link",
            expected: []Link{}, // Skip invalid
        },
        {
            name:    "slug with spaces",
            content: "Link with [[my note]] spaces",
            expected: []Link{{TargetSlug: "my-note"}}, // Normalized
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            links := ExtractWikiLinks(tt.content)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expected, links)
        })
    }
}
```

**Service layer tests**:
- Test `UpdateNoteLinks` creates links correctly
- Test `UpdateNoteLinks` deletes old links
- Test `ResolveOrphanedBacklinks` connects orphaned links
- Test duplicate link handling (UNIQUE constraint)
- Test NULL target_note_id for non-existent notes

- [ ] Unit tests for link extraction (>90% coverage)
- [ ] Integration tests for service layer
- [ ] Test edge cases thoroughly

### Edge Cases to Handle

1. **Malformed links**: `[[incomplete`, `[[double|pipe|text]]`
   - Skip, log warning

2. **Nested brackets**: `[[note-[[inner]]]]`
   - Match outer-most brackets only

3. **Very long slugs**: `[[slug-that-exceeds-maximum-length-of-100-characters...]]`
   - Truncate or skip

4. **Special characters in slug**: `[[note's-slug]]`, `[[note/with/slash]]`
   - Normalize: remove apostrophes, replace slashes with hyphens

5. **Case sensitivity**: `[[Note-Slug]]` vs `[[note-slug]]`
   - Always normalize to lowercase

6. **Unicode in slugs**: `[[café-notes]]`, `[[日本語]]`
   - Allow or transliterate to ASCII (decision needed)

7. **Link to same note**: `[[my-note]]` in `my-note.md`
   - Allow (some users do this intentionally)

8. **Circular links**: Note A → B, Note B → A
   - Allow (common in zettelkasten)

## Example Usage

**Creating a note with links**:
```go
note := &models.Note{
    Title: "Zettelkasten Method",
    Slug:  "zettelkasten-method",
    // ... other fields
}

content := `
The Zettelkasten method uses [[atomic-notes]] and [[permanent-notes|permanent notes]].
See also [[slip-box]] for the original concept.
`

// Save note
noteService.Create(note, content)

// Links are automatically extracted and stored:
// 1. zettelkasten-method → atomic-notes
// 2. zettelkasten-method → permanent-notes (display: "permanent notes")
// 3. zettelkasten-method → slip-box
```

**Querying backlinks**:
```go
// Find all notes linking to "atomic-notes"
var links []models.NoteLink
db.Where("target_slug = ?", "atomic-notes").
   Preload("SourceNote").
   Find(&links)

// Each link has:
// - SourceNote: The note containing the link
// - TargetSlug: "atomic-notes"
```

## Definition of Done
- [ ] `ExtractWikiLinks` function parses both link syntaxes
- [ ] Links extracted from note content correctly
- [ ] Links stored in `note_links` table with correct foreign keys
- [ ] `target_note_id` set when target exists, NULL otherwise
- [ ] Orphaned links resolved when target note created
- [ ] Code blocks and inline code excluded from link detection
- [ ] Slugs normalized (lowercase, spaces to hyphens)
- [ ] Invalid/empty links skipped gracefully
- [ ] Old links deleted when note updated
- [ ] Unit tests >90% coverage
- [ ] Integration tests pass
- [ ] Backlink queries work (find all notes linking to target)

## Time Estimate
3-4 hours (includes parsing, service integration, and comprehensive testing)

title: Implement notes pane to show linked notes and backlinks
description:
## Overview
Create a new TUI component that displays linked notes (outgoing links) and backlinks (incoming links) for the currently selected item (task, journal entry, or note). This component is essential for navigating the zettelkasten knowledge graph within the TUI.

## Background Context
- Zettelkasten relies on bidirectional links between notes
- Wiki-style links (`[[note-slug]]`) are detected and stored in `note_links` table (reckon-4pn)
- Each note can have outgoing links (notes it references) and backlinks (notes referencing it)
- This pane should update dynamically based on the currently focused item
- Must integrate with existing TUI layout and component architecture

## Acceptance Criteria

### 1. Component Interface

**File**: `internal/tui/components/notes_pane.go`

```go
package components

import (
    "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/viewport"
    tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
    "github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
)

// NotesPane displays linked notes and backlinks for selected item
type NotesPane struct {
    width      int
    height     int
    focused    bool
    
    // Content
    outgoingLinks []NoteLink  // Links from current item to other notes
    backlinks     []NoteLink  // Links from other notes to current item
    
    // UI state
    viewport      viewport.Model
    cursor        int         // Currently selected link
    section       Section     // OutgoingSection or BacklinksSection
    
    // Styling
    styles        Styles
}

type NoteLink struct {
    TargetSlug   string
    TargetTitle  string
    DisplayText  string  // Optional custom link text
    SourceSlug   string  // For backlinks: where the link came from
    SourceTitle  string  // For backlinks: source note title
}

type Section int
const (
    OutgoingSection Section = iota
    BacklinksSection
)

// Constructor
func NewNotesPane(width, height int) *NotesPane

// Update methods
func (np *NotesPane) Update(msg tea.Msg) (*NotesPane, tea.Cmd)
func (np *NotesPane) SetLinks(outgoing, backlinks []NoteLink)
func (np *NotesPane) SetFocus(focused bool)
func (np *NotesPane) SetSize(width, height int)

// Rendering
func (np *NotesPane) View() string

// Navigation
func (np *NotesPane) MoveUp()
func (np *NotesPane) MoveDown()
func (np *NotesPane) GetSelectedLink() *NoteLink
func (np *NotesPane) ToggleSection() // Switch between outgoing and backlinks
```

- [ ] Define NotesPane struct with all required fields
- [ ] Implement constructor with default styling
- [ ] Implement Update method for Bubbletea integration
- [ ] Implement SetLinks to update displayed links
- [ ] Implement SetFocus for focus management
- [ ] Implement View method for rendering

### 2. Link Data Fetching

**Service integration** (`internal/tui/model.go`):

```go
// Update notes pane when selection changes
func (m *Model) updateNotesForSelectedTask() {
    // Get selected task's associated note (if any)
    task := m.getSelectedTask()
    if task == nil || task.NoteSlug == "" {
        m.notesPane.SetLinks(nil, nil)
        return
    }
    
    // Fetch links from database
    outgoing := m.noteService.GetOutgoingLinks(task.NoteSlug)
    backlinks := m.noteService.GetBacklinks(task.NoteSlug)
    
    m.notesPane.SetLinks(outgoing, backlinks)
}

// Service methods
func (ns *NoteService) GetOutgoingLinks(slug string) []NoteLink {
    var links []NoteLink
    ns.db.Raw(`
        SELECT 
            nl.target_slug,
            n.title as target_title,
            nl.display_text
        FROM note_links nl
        LEFT JOIN notes n ON nl.target_note_id = n.id
        WHERE nl.source_note_id = (
            SELECT id FROM notes WHERE slug = ?
        )
    `, slug).Scan(&links)
    return links
}

func (ns *NoteService) GetBacklinks(slug string) []NoteLink {
    var links []NoteLink
    ns.db.Raw(`
        SELECT 
            nl.source_note_id,
            sn.slug as source_slug,
            sn.title as source_title
        FROM note_links nl
        JOIN notes sn ON nl.source_note_id = sn.id
        WHERE nl.target_slug = ?
    `, slug).Scan(&links)
    return links
}
```

- [ ] Implement `GetOutgoingLinks` in NoteService
- [ ] Implement `GetBacklinks` in NoteService
- [ ] Call update method when task selection changes
- [ ] Call update method when journal entry selected
- [ ] Call update method when note selected

### 3. Component Layout

**Visual design**:

```
╭─ Linked Notes ────────────────╮
│                               │
│ OUTGOING (3)                  │
│ ├─ atomic-notes               │
│ ├─ permanent-notes            │
│ └─ slip-box                   │
│                               │
│ BACKLINKS (2)                 │
│ ├─ from: learning-techniques  │
│ └─ from: note-taking-methods  │
│                               │
╰───────────────────────────────╯
```

**With cursor**:
```
╭─ Linked Notes ────────────────╮
│                               │
│ OUTGOING (3)                  │
│ ├─ atomic-notes               │
│ ▶ permanent-notes             │  ← cursor
│ └─ slip-box                   │
│                               │
│ BACKLINKS (2)                 │
│ ├─ from: learning-techniques  │
│ └─ from: note-taking-methods  │
╰───────────────────────────────╯
```

**When focused**:
```
╭─ Linked Notes ────────────────╮  ← highlighted border
│                               │
│ OUTGOING (3)                  │
│ ├─ atomic-notes               │
│ ▶ permanent-notes             │  ← bright cursor
│ └─ slip-box                   │
│                               │
│ BACKLINKS (2)                 │
│ ├─ from: learning-techniques  │
│ └─ from: note-taking-methods  │
╰───────────────────────────────╯
```

**Styling requirements**:
- [ ] Border with title "Linked Notes"
- [ ] Section headers: "OUTGOING (N)" and "BACKLINKS (N)"
- [ ] Tree-like display with ├─ and └─ characters
- [ ] Cursor indicator (▶) for selected link
- [ ] Dimmed style when not focused
- [ ] Highlighted border when focused
- [ ] Empty state message when no links

### 4. Keyboard Navigation

**Within NotesPane** (when focused):
- `j` / `↓` - Move cursor down
- `k` / `↑` - Move cursor up
- `enter` - Follow selected link (open note)
- `tab` - Toggle between OUTGOING and BACKLINKS sections
- `g` - Jump to top
- `G` - Jump to bottom

**From main TUI**:
- `tab` / `shift+tab` - Cycle focus to/from notes pane
- Focus behavior integrates with existing pane navigation

**Implementation**:
```go
func (np *NotesPane) Update(msg tea.Msg) (*NotesPane, tea.Cmd) {
    if !np.focused {
        return np, nil
    }
    
    switch msg := msg.(type) {
    case tea.KeyMsg:
        switch msg.String() {
        case "j", "down":
            np.MoveDown()
        case "k", "up":
            np.MoveUp()
        case "enter":
            return np, np.followLink()
        case "tab":
            np.ToggleSection()
        case "g":
            np.cursor = 0
        case "G":
            np.cursor = np.getTotalLinks() - 1
        }
    }
    
    return np, nil
}

func (np *NotesPane) MoveDown() {
    totalLinks := np.getTotalLinks()
    if totalLinks == 0 {
        return
    }
    np.cursor = min(np.cursor+1, totalLinks-1)
}

func (np *NotesPane) getTotalLinks() int {
    return len(np.outgoingLinks) + len(np.backlinks)
}
```

- [ ] Implement keyboard navigation (j/k, arrows)
- [ ] Implement enter to follow link
- [ ] Implement tab to toggle sections
- [ ] Implement g/G for jump to top/bottom
- [ ] Handle empty state (no links)

### 5. Link Following

When user presses `enter` on a link:

```go
// Message to notify parent that link was selected
type LinkSelectedMsg struct {
    Slug  string
    Title string
}

func (np *NotesPane) followLink() tea.Cmd {
    link := np.GetSelectedLink()
    if link == nil {
        return nil
    }
    
    return func() tea.Msg {
        return LinkSelectedMsg{
            Slug:  link.TargetSlug,
            Title: link.TargetTitle,
        }
    }
}
```

**Parent handler** (`internal/tui/model.go`):
```go
case LinkSelectedMsg:
    // Switch to note view mode
    m.mode = ModeNotes
    m.currentNoteSlug = msg.Slug
    
    // Load note content
    return m, m.loadNote(msg.Slug)
```

- [ ] Emit LinkSelectedMsg when enter pressed
- [ ] Handle in parent model to navigate to note
- [ ] Maintain navigation history (for back button)
- [ ] Update notes pane with new note's links

### 6. Empty State Handling

**No outgoing links**:
```
╭─ Linked Notes ────────────────╮
│                               │
│ OUTGOING (0)                  │
│   (no outgoing links)         │
│                               │
│ BACKLINKS (2)                 │
│ ├─ from: learning-techniques  │
│ └─ from: note-taking-methods  │
╰───────────────────────────────╯
```

**No backlinks**:
```
╭─ Linked Notes ────────────────╮
│                               │
│ OUTGOING (3)                  │
│ ├─ atomic-notes               │
│ ├─ permanent-notes            │
│ └─ slip-box                   │
│                               │
│ BACKLINKS (0)                 │
│   (no backlinks)              │
╰───────────────────────────────╯
```

**No links at all**:
```
╭─ Linked Notes ────────────────╮
│                               │
│     No linked notes yet       │
│                               │
│  Create links using [[slug]]  │
│     in your note content      │
│                               │
╰───────────────────────────────╯
```

**No note associated**:
```
╭─ Linked Notes ────────────────╮
│                               │
│    No note for this item      │
│                               │
╰───────────────────────────────╯
```

- [ ] Show helpful message for empty states
- [ ] Hide sections when no links
- [ ] Disable navigation when empty
- [ ] Show usage hint for creating links

### 7. Integration with Main TUI

**Layout** (example):
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    Journal View                         │
│  ┌──────────┬──────────────┬──────────┬──────────────┐ │
│  │Intentions│     Logs     │   Wins   │ Linked Notes │ │
│  │          │              │          │              │ │
│  │  1. ...  │   10:00 AM   │  ✓ ...   │ OUTGOING (3) │ │
│  │  2. ...  │   Meeting    │  ✓ ...   │ • note-1     │ │
│  │  3. ...  │              │          │ • note-2     │ │
│  │          │   2:00 PM    │          │              │ │
│  │          │   Code review│          │ BACKLINKS(1) │ │
│  │          │              │          │ • from: ...  │ │
│  └──────────┴──────────────┴──────────┴──────────────┘ │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Pane placement options**:
1. **Right sidebar** (recommended) - Dedicated vertical pane on right
2. **Bottom drawer** - Expandable bottom panel
3. **Floating overlay** - Modal-style overlay when triggered

**Recommended: Right sidebar**
```go
// In model.go
type Model struct {
    // ... existing fields
    notesPane *components.NotesPane
}

func (m *Model) View() string {
    if m.notesPane == nil {
        return m.renderMainView()
    }
    
    // Render with notes pane on right
    mainView := m.renderMainView()
    notesView := m.notesPane.View()
    
    return lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(
        lipgloss.Top,
        mainView,
        notesView,
    )
}
```

### 8. Styling

**Color scheme**:
```go
type Styles struct {
    Border         lipgloss.Style
    BorderFocused  lipgloss.Style
    Title          lipgloss.Style
    SectionHeader  lipgloss.Style
    Link           lipgloss.Style
    LinkFocused    lipgloss.Style
    Cursor         lipgloss.Style
    Empty          lipgloss.Style
}

func DefaultStyles() Styles {
    return Styles{
        Border: lipgloss.NewStyle().
            Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
            BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("240")),
        BorderFocused: lipgloss.NewStyle().
            Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
            BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("62")),  // Blue
        SectionHeader: lipgloss.NewStyle().
            Foreground(lipgloss.Color("243")).
            Bold(true),
        Link: lipgloss.NewStyle().
            Foreground(lipgloss.Color("252")),
        LinkFocused: lipgloss.NewStyle().
            Foreground(lipgloss.Color("15")).
            Bold(true),
        Cursor: lipgloss.NewStyle().

```

- [ ] Define comprehensive style struct
- [ ] Use Lipgloss for all styling
- [ ] Support focused/unfocused states
- [ ] Use consistent colors with rest of TUI
- [ ] Make styles configurable (optional)

## Implementation Guidance

### File Structure
```
internal/tui/components/
  notes_pane.go         # NEW: NotesPane component
  notes_pane_test.go    # NEW: Unit tests
internal/service/
  note_service.go       # MODIFY: Add GetOutgoingLinks, GetBacklinks
internal/tui/
  model.go              # MODIFY: Add NotesPane, handle LinkSelectedMsg
```

### Component Architecture

Follow existing component patterns (see TaskList, LogView):
- Implement Bubbletea Update interface
- Use viewport for scrolling if needed
- Maintain internal state (cursor, focus)
- Emit messages for parent to handle
- Style with Lipgloss

### Database Queries

**Efficient backlink query**:
```sql
-- Get all notes linking to a specific note
SELECT 
    sn.slug as source_slug,
    sn.title as source_title,
    nl.target_slug
FROM note_links nl
JOIN notes sn ON nl.source_note_id = sn.id
WHERE nl.target_slug = ?
ORDER BY sn.title
```

**Outgoing links with orphaned handling**:
```sql
-- Get all links from a note (including orphaned)
SELECT 
    nl.target_slug,
    COALESCE(tn.title, nl.target_slug) as target_title
FROM note_links nl
LEFT JOIN notes tn ON nl.target_note_id = tn.id
WHERE nl.source_note_id = (SELECT id FROM notes WHERE slug = ?)
ORDER BY nl.target_slug
```

- [ ] Use efficient SQL queries with joins
- [ ] Handle orphaned links gracefully
- [ ] Cache results to avoid repeated queries
- [ ] Update cache when links change

### Testing Requirements

**Unit tests**:
```go
func TestNotesPaneNavigation(t *testing.T) {
    np := NewNotesPane(40, 20)
    np.SetLinks(
        []NoteLink{
            {TargetSlug: "note-1", TargetTitle: "Note 1"},
            {TargetSlug: "note-2", TargetTitle: "Note 2"},
        },
        []NoteLink{
            {SourceSlug: "note-3", SourceTitle: "Note 3"},
        },
    )
    
    // Test navigation
    assert.Equal(t, 0, np.cursor)
    np.MoveDown()
    assert.Equal(t, 1, np.cursor)
    np.MoveDown()
    assert.Equal(t, 2, np.cursor)  // Backlinks section
    
    // Test bounds
    np.MoveUp()
    np.MoveUp()
    np.MoveUp()
    assert.Equal(t, 0, np.cursor)  // Can't go below 0
}

func TestNotesPaneEmptyState(t *testing.T) {
    np := NewNotesPane(40, 20)
    np.SetLinks(nil, nil)
    
    view := np.View()
    assert.Contains(t, view, "No linked notes")
}
```

**Integration tests**:
- Create note with links
- Render NotesPane
- Verify links displayed
- Test navigation and link following

- [ ] Unit tests for navigation
- [ ] Unit tests for section toggle
- [ ] Unit tests for empty states
- [ ] Integration tests with database
- [ ] Test focus management

### Edge Cases

1. **Orphaned links**: Target note doesn't exist
   - Display target_slug, show "(not found)" indicator
   
2. **Very long titles**: Truncate with ellipsis
   - Max display length: `width - 10` characters
   
3. **Many links**: More than fit in viewport
   - Use scrolling viewport
   - Show scroll indicator
   
4. **Circular links**: Note A → B, B → A
   - Allow (valid in zettelkasten)
   - Show both directions
   
5. **Self-links**: Note links to itself
   - Allow, show in outgoing section

6. **No note associated**: Task/log without note
   - Show empty state message
   
7. **Rapid selection changes**: Debounce updates
   - Don't query on every cursor move
   - Update on focus change or enter

## Example Usage

**User workflow**:
1. User opens TUI in journal mode
2. User selects a log entry that has associated note
3. Notes pane updates to show linked notes
4. User tabs to notes pane
5. User navigates with j/k to desired link
6. User presses enter to follow link
7. TUI switches to note view mode
8. Notes pane updates to show new note's links

## Definition of Done

- [ ] NotesPane component implemented with full interface
- [ ] Displays outgoing links and backlinks separately
- [ ] Section headers show count: "OUTGOING (N)", "BACKLINKS (N)"
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works (j/k, enter, tab, g/G)
- [ ] Cursor visible and updates correctly
- [ ] Focused/unfocused styling implemented
- [ ] Empty states handled gracefully
- [ ] LinkSelectedMsg emitted on enter
- [ ] Integrated into main TUI layout
- [ ] GetOutgoingLinks and GetBacklinks service methods implemented
- [ ] Updates when selection changes
- [ ] Unit tests pass (>80% coverage)
- [ ] Integration tests pass
- [ ] Manual testing confirms functionality
- [ ] Follows existing component patterns

## Time Estimate
4-5 hours (includes component implementation, service integration, testing)

## References
- Existing components: `internal/tui/components/task_list.go`, `internal/tui/components/log_view.go`
- Bubbletea viewport: https://github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/tree/master/viewport
- Lipgloss styling: https://github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss

title: Create zettelkasten notes directory structure and schema
description:
## Overview
Establish the foundational directory structure and database schema for the zettelkasten notes system. This is the base layer that all other note-related features will build upon.

## Background Context
- Zettelkasten is a note-taking method focused on atomic notes with bidirectional links
- Notes are standalone (not tied to tasks/projects) and live in `~/.reckon/notes/`
- Each note is a markdown file with frontmatter + content
- Links between notes use wiki-style syntax: `[[note-slug]]` or `[[note-slug|display text]]`
- Must integrate with existing reckon architecture (GORM models, service pattern, repository pattern)

## Acceptance Criteria

### 1. Directory Structure
Create the following directory structure:
```
~/.reckon/notes/           # All note markdown files live here
~/.reckon/notes/.meta/     # Optional: For future note metadata/indexes
```

- [ ] Create `~/.reckon/notes/` if it doesn't exist (on `rk init` or first note creation)
- [ ] Set appropriate permissions (0755 for dirs, 0644 for files)
- [ ] Document directory structure in code comments

### 2. Database Schema

#### notes table
```sql
CREATE TABLE notes (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    slug TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,  -- URL-friendly identifier (e.g., "my-note-title")
    file_path TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,  -- Relative path: "my-note-title.md"
    created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    tags TEXT,  -- JSON array: ["tag1", "tag2"] - for future use
    
    -- Indexes for common queries
    CREATE INDEX idx_notes_slug ON notes(slug);
    CREATE INDEX idx_notes_created_at ON notes(created_at);
    CREATE INDEX idx_notes_updated_at ON notes(updated_at);
);
```

#### note_links table
```sql
CREATE TABLE note_links (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    source_note_id INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- The note containing the link
    target_slug TEXT NOT NULL,        -- The slug being linked to (may not exist yet)
    target_note_id INTEGER,            -- NULL if target note doesn't exist yet
    link_type TEXT DEFAULT 'wiki',    -- 'wiki' for [[links]], future: 'embed', 'reference'
    created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    
    FOREIGN KEY (source_note_id) REFERENCES notes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    FOREIGN KEY (target_note_id) REFERENCES notes(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    
    -- Prevent duplicate links from same source to same target
    UNIQUE(source_note_id, target_slug),
    
    -- Indexes for efficient backlink queries
    CREATE INDEX idx_note_links_source ON note_links(source_note_id);
    CREATE INDEX idx_note_links_target_id ON note_links(target_note_id);
    CREATE INDEX idx_note_links_target_slug ON note_links(target_slug);
);
```

- [ ] Create migration file: `internal/db/migrations/XXXXXX_create_notes_tables.sql`
- [ ] Follow existing migration numbering scheme
- [ ] Test migration runs successfully (up and down)
- [ ] Verify indexes created correctly

### 3. GORM Models

Create in `internal/models/note.go`:

```go
package models

import (
    "time"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Note represents a zettelkasten note
type Note struct {
    ID        uint           `gorm:"primaryKey" json:"id"`
    Title     string         `gorm:"not null" json:"title"`
    Slug      string         `gorm:"uniqueIndex;not null" json:"slug"`
    FilePath  string         `gorm:"uniqueIndex;not null" json:"file_path"`
    CreatedAt time.Time      `json:"created_at"`
    UpdatedAt time.Time      `json:"updated_at"`
    DeletedAt gorm.DeletedAt `gorm:"index" json:"-"`
    Tags      string         `json:"tags"` // JSON array stored as string
    
    // Associations (loaded separately)
    OutgoingLinks []NoteLink `gorm:"foreignKey:SourceNoteID" json:"outgoing_links,omitempty"`
    IncomingLinks []NoteLink `gorm:"foreignKey:TargetNoteID" json:"incoming_links,omitempty"`
}

// NoteLink represents a link from one note to another
type NoteLink struct {
    ID           uint       `gorm:"primaryKey" json:"id"`
    SourceNoteID uint       `gorm:"not null;index" json:"source_note_id"`
    TargetSlug   string     `gorm:"not null;index" json:"target_slug"`
    TargetNoteID *uint      `gorm:"index" json:"target_note_id"` // Pointer for nullable
    LinkType     string     `gorm:"default:'wiki'" json:"link_type"`
    CreatedAt    time.Time  `json:"created_at"`
    
    // Associations
    SourceNote   *Note      `gorm:"foreignKey:SourceNoteID" json:"source_note,omitempty"`
    TargetNote   *Note      `gorm:"foreignKey:TargetNoteID" json:"target_note,omitempty"`
}

// TableName overrides (if needed)
func (Note) TableName() string {
    return "notes"
}

func (NoteLink) TableName() string {
    return "note_links"
}
```

- [ ] Add models to `internal/models/note.go`
- [ ] Follow existing model patterns (see task.go, log_entry.go for reference)
- [ ] Add validation tags if needed
- [ ] Include JSON tags for future API use

### 4. Note File Format

Each note file should follow this markdown format:

```markdown
---
title: "My Note Title"
slug: "my-note-title"
created_at: 2026-01-18T10:30:00Z
updated_at: 2026-01-18T10:30:00Z
tags: ["tag1", "tag2"]
---

Note content goes here. You can use [[other-note]] to link to other notes.

You can also use [[note-slug|custom display text]] for links.
```

- [ ] Document this format in code comments
- [ ] This format will be enforced by the note creation service (future ticket)

### 5. Database Integration

Update `internal/db/db.go`:
- [ ] Add Note and NoteLink to AutoMigrate call
- [ ] Ensure migrations run in correct order
- [ ] Add any necessary indexes

Example:
```go
err := db.AutoMigrate(
    &models.Task{},
    &models.LogEntry{},
    &models.Note{},        // Add this
    &models.NoteLink{},    // Add this
)
```

### 6. Validation

- [ ] Test migration runs cleanly on fresh database
- [ ] Test migration runs cleanly on existing database (with tasks/logs)
- [ ] Verify foreign key constraints work (CASCADE on delete)
- [ ] Verify unique constraints (slug, file_path)
- [ ] Test that duplicate source->target links are prevented

## Implementation Guidance

### File Organization
```
internal/
  models/
    note.go           # NEW: Note and NoteLink models
  db/
    migrations/
      XXXXXX_create_notes_tables.sql  # NEW: Migration file
    db.go             # MODIFY: Add AutoMigrate for notes
```

### Migration File Naming
- Follow existing pattern: `XXXXXX_create_notes_tables.sql`
- Check `internal/db/migrations/` for the next sequence number
- Use descriptive name indicating what the migration does

### GORM Patterns to Follow

**Foreign Keys**: Use `gorm:"foreignKey:FieldName"` tag
**Indexes**: Use `gorm:"index"` or `gorm:"uniqueIndex"`
**Nullable Foreign Keys**: Use pointer type (`*uint`)
**Timestamps**: GORM handles created_at/updated_at automatically
**Soft Deletes**: Use `gorm.DeletedAt` type

### Testing Requirements

Create `internal/models/note_test.go`:
- [ ] Test Note model creation and retrieval
- [ ] Test slug uniqueness constraint
- [ ] Test NoteLink creation with valid source_note_id
- [ ] Test NoteLink cascade delete (delete note deletes its links)
- [ ] Test NoteLink SET NULL (delete target note sets target_note_id to NULL)
- [ ] Test duplicate link prevention (same source->target)
- [ ] Test backlink queries (find all notes linking to a given note)

Example test structure:
```go
func TestNoteCreation(t *testing.T) {
    db := setupTestDB(t)
    
    note := &models.Note{
        Title: "Test Note",
        Slug: "test-note",
        FilePath: "test-note.md",
    }
    
    err := db.Create(note).Error
    assert.NoError(t, err)
    assert.NotZero(t, note.ID)
}
```

### Edge Cases to Consider

1. **Orphaned Links**: TargetNoteID can be NULL if target doesn't exist yet
   - This is intentional! Users can create `[[future-note]]` links before the note exists
   - When future-note is created, backfill the target_note_id

2. **Circular Links**: Note A links to B, B links to A
   - This is valid in zettelkasten, no special handling needed

3. **Self Links**: Note links to itself
   - Allow this (some zettelkasten users do this intentionally)

4. **Slug Collisions**: Two notes with same title
   - Will be handled by note creation service (future ticket)
   - For now, just enforce UNIQUE constraint at DB level

5. **Tags Format**: Stored as JSON string for simplicity
   - Use `[]` for no tags (empty JSON array)
   - Validation happens at service layer (future ticket)

## Integration Points

### For Future Tickets
This schema enables:
- `reckon-2pq`: Will use Note model to create new notes
- `reckon-4pn`: Will populate note_links table when parsing wiki links
- `reckon-gzr`: Will query notes table for full-text search

### Existing Code
- No existing code depends on this (net new feature)
- Ensure migration doesn't break existing task/log functionality

## Definition of Done
- [ ] `~/.reckon/notes/` directory exists after `rk init`
- [ ] Migration file creates both tables with correct schema
- [ ] Note and NoteLink models defined in internal/models/note.go
- [ ] Models integrated into db.AutoMigrate
- [ ] All foreign key constraints work correctly
- [ ] All unique constraints work correctly
- [ ] Unit tests pass with >80% coverage
- [ ] Can create a Note and NoteLink manually via GORM (in test)
- [ ] Backlink queries work (find notes linking to a given note)
- [ ] Migration is reversible (down migration works)

## Time Estimate
3-4 hours (includes schema design, model creation, migration testing, unit tests)

## References
- Existing patterns: See `internal/models/task.go`, `internal/models/log_entry.go`
- Migration examples: See `internal/db/migrations/`
- GORM docs: https://gorm.io/docs/models.html

title: Create data migration tool for ID system changes
description:
## Overview
Build a migration tool to convert existing reckon data files from opaque ID-based format to the new slug-based and date-prefixed format. This migration is critical for the ID System Improvements epic and must handle edge cases gracefully.

## Background Context
- **Old format**: Task files use slugs (e.g., `20260115-fix-bug.md`), log entry markdown uses opaque IDs in filenames (e.g., `abc123.md`)
- **New format**: Both use slug-based filenames with date prefix (e.g., `20260115-fix-bug.md`, `20260115-daily-standup.md`)
- This tool must run once per installation to migrate existing data
- See reckon-0ok and reckon-mo6 for details on the new format

## Acceptance Criteria

### 1. Backup Creation
- [ ] Create timestamped backup directory: `~/.reckon/backups/migration-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS/`
- [ ] Copy entire `~/.reckon/` directory contents to backup before any modifications
- [ ] Verify backup integrity (file count matches, sizes reasonable)
- [ ] Print backup location to user with instructions for rollback

### 2. Task File Migration
- [ ] Scan `~/.reckon/tasks/` for all `.md` files
- [ ] For files not already in `YYYYMMDD-slug.md` format:
  - Extract date from frontmatter `created_at` field
  - Generate slug from title (lowercase, hyphens, max 50 chars)
  - Rename to `YYYYMMDD-slug.md` format
- [ ] Update database `tasks` table `file_path` column to match new filenames
- [ ] Validate: All task file_paths in DB match actual files on disk

### 3. Log Entry Markdown Migration
- [ ] Scan `~/.reckon/log/` for all `.md` files
- [ ] For files using opaque ID format (not matching `YYYYMMDD-*.md`):
  - Query database to find log entry's `logged_at` date and `title`
  - Generate slug from title
  - Rename to `YYYYMMDD-slug.md` format
- [ ] Update database `log_entries` table `file_path` column
- [ ] Handle collisions: If slug already exists, append `-2`, `-3`, etc.
- [ ] Validate: All log entry file_paths in DB match actual files on disk

### 4. Validation & Verification
- [ ] Count files before migration (tasks and log entries separately)
- [ ] Count files after migration - numbers must match
- [ ] Verify no orphaned files (files not referenced in DB)
- [ ] Verify no missing files (DB references non-existent files)
- [ ] Check for duplicate slugs - fail migration if found
- [ ] Print summary report:
  ```
  Migration Summary:
  - Tasks migrated: X/Y
  - Log entries migrated: X/Y
  - Backup location: ~/.reckon/backups/...
  - Status: SUCCESS | FAILED
  ```

### 5. Error Handling
- [ ] If ANY validation fails, halt before making changes
- [ ] If error during migration, print rollback instructions
- [ ] Provide rollback command: `rk migrate rollback <backup-id>`
- [ ] Log all operations to `~/.reckon/migration.log`
- [ ] Transaction-like behavior: Either all migrations succeed or none do (within each category)

### 6. User Experience
- [ ] Add `rk migrate run` command
- [ ] Add `rk migrate status` to check if migration needed
- [ ] Add `rk migrate rollback <backup-id>` to restore from backup
- [ ] Show progress indicator for large migrations (>50 files)
- [ ] Require explicit confirmation before running (show what will change)
- [ ] Make migration idempotent (safe to run multiple times)

## Implementation Guidance

### File Structure
Create in `internal/migrate/`:
- `migrate.go` - Main migration orchestrator
- `backup.go` - Backup creation and rollback
- `task_files.go` - Task file migration logic
- `log_files.go` - Log entry file migration logic
- `validate.go` - Pre/post validation checks

### Key Patterns to Follow

**Slug Generation** (must match existing service layer):
```go
// Use the same slug generation as in service layer
// Check internal/service/task_service.go for the canonical implementation
func generateSlug(title string) string {
    // Lowercase, replace spaces with hyphens, remove special chars
    // Truncate to reasonable length (50 chars)
}
```

**Date Formatting**:
```go
// Use format: YYYYMMDD (e.g., 20260115)
datePrefix := logEntry.LoggedAt.Format("20060102")
```

**Database Updates**:
```go
// Update file_path in transaction
tx := db.Begin()
defer tx.Rollback()

// ... perform updates ...

if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
    // Handle error, print rollback instructions
}
```

### Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for slug generation (matches service layer)
- [ ] Unit tests for date prefix formatting
- [ ] Integration test with sample data (5 tasks, 5 log entries)
- [ ] Test collision handling (duplicate slugs)
- [ ] Test rollback functionality
- [ ] Test idempotency (run twice, no errors second time)

### Edge Cases to Handle
1. **Missing frontmatter**: If `created_at` missing, use file modification time
2. **Invalid characters in title**: Strip or replace (parentheses, slashes, etc.)
3. **Very long titles**: Truncate slug intelligently (at word boundary)
4. **Empty titles**: Use fallback like `untitled-<timestamp>`
5. **Duplicate slugs on same date**: Append numeric suffix
6. **Already migrated files**: Skip silently (idempotent behavior)
7. **Database out of sync**: Fail validation, don't attempt migration

## Documentation Requirements
- [ ] Add migration guide to docs/ explaining when and how to run
- [ ] Document rollback procedure
- [ ] Add troubleshooting section for common issues
- [ ] Include example migration output in docs

## Definition of Done
- [ ] `rk migrate run` successfully converts all files
- [ ] All validation checks pass
- [ ] Backup created and verified
- [ ] Database file_path columns updated correctly
- [ ] No orphaned or missing files
- [ ] Tests passing with >80% coverage
- [ ] Documentation complete
- [ ] Tested on real reckon installation with existing data

## Time Estimate
3-4 hours (includes testing and documentation)
close_reason: Already implemented in commits e1c72ae, af96b60, 6fee0af and merged to main

title: Epic: Code Quality & Refactoring
Add keyboard shortcuts to open items in external editor
description:
Add 'e' or 'E' keyboard shortcuts to open journals, tasks, and notes in $EDITOR from TUI.

## Overview

Users should be able to quickly edit any item in their preferred editor without leaving the TUI. After editor closes, TUI reloads the modified content.

## Keyboard Shortcut

**'e' or 'E' key** when focused on item:
- Journal view: Opens current day's journal file
- Task: Opens task markdown file
- Note: Opens note markdown file
- Intention/Win/Log: Opens parent journal file with cursor hint

## Implementation

### Editor Helper

**File**: `internal/tui/editor.go`

```go
func OpenInEditor(filePath string) error {
    // Get $EDITOR env var (default to vi/nano/vim)
    editor := os.Getenv("EDITOR")
    if editor == "" {
        editor = findAvailableEditor() // Try vi, nano, vim
    }
    
    // Run editor as subprocess
    cmd := exec.Command(editor, filePath)
    cmd.Stdin = os.Stdin
    cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
    cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
    
    // Run and wait
    return cmd.Run()
}

func findAvailableEditor() string {
    editors := []string{"vi", "vim", "nano", "emacs"}
    for _, ed := range editors {
        if _, err := exec.LookPath(ed); err == nil {
            return ed
        }
    }
    return "vi" // Fallback
}
```

### TUI Integration

**File**: `internal/tui/model.go`

Handle 'e' key in different contexts:

**Journal View**:
```go
case "e", "E":
    if m.focusedSection == SectionJournal {
        filePath := m.journal.FilePath
        m.Suspend() // Suspend TUI
        OpenInEditor(filePath)
        m.Resume()  // Resume TUI
        m.ReloadJournal() // Reload after edit
    }
```

**Task List**:
```go
case "e", "E":
    if m.focusedSection == SectionTasks {
        task := m.getSelectedTask()
        filePath := filepath.Join(tasksDir, task.ID + ".md")
        m.Suspend()
        OpenInEditor(filePath)
        m.Resume()
        m.ReloadTask(task.ID)
    }
```

**Note View**:
```go
case "e", "E":
    if m.mode == ModeNotes && m.notesMode == NotesModeView {
        filePath := m.noteView.Note.FilePath
        m.Suspend()
        OpenInEditor(filePath)
        m.Resume()
        m.ReloadNote(m.noteView.Note.Slug)
    }
```

### Suspend/Resume TUI

**File**: `internal/tui/model.go`

```go
func (m *Model) Suspend() tea.Cmd {
    // Exit alternate screen
    // Restore cursor
    // Clear screen
    return tea.ExitAltScreen
}

func (m *Model) Resume() tea.Cmd {
    // Re-enter alternate screen
    // Redraw UI
    return tea.EnterAltScreen
}
```

### Reload After Edit

After editor closes, reload the file:

**Journal**:
```go
func (m *Model) ReloadJournal() {
    journal, err := m.journalService.GetByDate(m.currentDate)
    if err != nil {
        m.showError("Failed to reload journal")
        return
    }
    m.journal = journal
    m.showMessage("Journal reloaded")
}
```

**Task**:
```go
func (m *Model) ReloadTask(id string) {
    // Re-parse task file
    // Update in task list
    // Re-index if needed
}
```

**Note**:
```go
func (m *Model) ReloadNote(slug string) {
    note, err := m.noteService.GetBySlug(slug)
    if err != nil {
        m.showError("Failed to reload note")
        return
    }
    m.noteView.Note = note
    m.showMessage("Note reloaded")
}
```

## Status Bar Updates

Show 'e' shortcut in relevant contexts:

**Journal mode**:
```
↑↓:nav  l:log  i:intention  w:win  e:edit  h/l:day  q:quit
```

**Task list**:
```
↑↓:nav  space:toggle  enter:expand  e:edit  d:delete  q:quit
```

**Note view**:
```
↑↓:scroll  enter:follow  e:edit  b:back  q:quit
```

## Error Handling

**Editor not found**:
- Show error: "No editor found. Set $EDITOR environment variable."
- Provide fallback suggestions

**Editor returns error**:
- Show error message
- Don't reload (keep original state)

**File modified externally while editing**:
- Detect file watcher notification
- Prompt user: "File changed. Reload? [y/n]"

## Acceptance Criteria

- 'e' key opens journal file in $EDITOR
- 'e' key opens task file in $EDITOR
- 'e' key opens note file in $EDITOR
- TUI suspends while editor is open
- TUI resumes after editor closes
- Content reloads automatically after edit
- Works with different editors (vim, nano, emacs)
- Falls back to vi if $EDITOR not set
- Status bar shows 'e' hint in relevant contexts
- Error handling for missing editor
- Success message after reload

## Configuration (Optional)

Allow editor preference in config:
```yaml
editor: vim
editor_args: ["-c", "set wrap"]
```

## Testing

Test with different editors:
- vim
- nano
- emacs
- code (VS Code - may need special handling)
- $EDITOR not set

Test editing:
- Journal file
- Task file
- Note file
- Multiple edits in same session

## Dependencies

None (standalone feature)

## Related

- Integrates with: reckon-5q5 (Notes mode)
- Works with existing: Journal, Tasks

title: Integrate time-grouped view with task details pane positioning
description:
Implement smart positioning for task details pane based on selected task's section.

## Context

When the task details pane (reckon-egt) is implemented, it should show details without covering the selected task. The positioning logic depends on which time-grouped section contains the selected task.

## Positioning Rules

**Default behavior**:
- Details pane covers/replaces the bottom section (ALL TASKS)
- Works when selected task is in TODAY or THIS WEEK sections

**Exception**:
- If selected task IS in the ALL TASKS section
- Details pane shows in the middle section (THIS WEEK area)
- This keeps the selected task visible

## Layout Examples

### Task in TODAY section (default)
```
╭─ TODAY (3) ────────────────────╮
│ • Fix critical bug ← SELECTED  │
│ • Review PR #123               │
╰────────────────────────────────╯

╭─ THIS WEEK (5) ────────────────╮
│ • Write docs                   │
│ • Code review                  │
╰────────────────────────────────╯

╭─ TASK DETAILS ─────────────────╮  ← Replaces ALL TASKS section
│ Fix critical bug               │
│                                │
│ Notes:                         │
│ - Broke in production          │
│ - Rollback planned             │
╰────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Task in ALL TASKS section (exception)
```
╭─ TODAY (3) ────────────────────╮
│ • Fix critical bug             │
│ • Review PR #123               │
╰────────────────────────────────╯

╭─ TASK DETAILS ─────────────────╮  ← Replaces THIS WEEK section
│ Future project planning        │
│                                │
│ Notes:                         │
│ - Research phase               │
│ - Due next quarter             │
╰────────────────────────────────╯

╭─ ALL TASKS (42) ───────────────╮
│ • Future project... ← SELECTED │
│ • Unscheduled task             │
╰────────────────────────────────╯
```

## Implementation

**File**: `internal/tui/model.go`

Add field to track detail pane position:
```go
type Model struct {
    // ... existing fields
    detailPanePosition DetailPanePosition
}

type DetailPanePosition int

const (
    DetailPaneBottom DetailPanePosition = iota  // Replace ALL TASKS
    DetailPaneMiddle                             // Replace THIS WEEK
)
```

**Calculate position**:
```go
func (m *Model) updateDetailPanePosition() {
    selectedTask := m.getSelectedTask()
    if selectedTask == nil {
        return
    }
    
    // Determine which section contains selected task
    section := m.getTaskSection(selectedTask)
    
    if section == SectionAllTasks {
        m.detailPanePosition = DetailPaneMiddle
    } else {
        m.detailPanePosition = DetailPaneBottom
    }
}
```

**Rendering**:
```go
func (m Model) View() string {
    // Render TODAY section (always)
    todayView := m.renderTodaySection()
    
    // Render middle section (THIS WEEK or DETAILS)
    var middleView string
    if m.showTaskDetails && m.detailPanePosition == DetailPaneMiddle {
        middleView = m.renderTaskDetails()
    } else {
        middleView = m.renderThisWeekSection()
    }
    
    // Render bottom section (ALL TASKS or DETAILS)
    var bottomView string
    if m.showTaskDetails && m.detailPanePosition == DetailPaneBottom {
        bottomView = m.renderTaskDetails()
    } else {
        bottomView = m.renderAllTasksSection()
    }
    
    return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, todayView, middleView, bottomView)
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- When task in TODAY selected, details show in bottom area
- When task in THIS WEEK selected, details show in bottom area
- When task in ALL TASKS selected, details show in middle area
- Selected task always remains visible when details shown
- Position updates when navigating between tasks
- Position updates when task moves sections (after scheduling)
- Smooth transitions between positions

## Dependencies

- Requires: reckon-3wq (Time-grouped task view)
- Integrates with: reckon-egt (Task details pane - may not be implemented yet)

## Notes

This ticket sets up the positioning logic. If reckon-egt (task details pane) is not yet implemented, this ticket should:
1. Create the positioning logic framework
2. Add placeholder rendering for testing
3. Make it easy to plug in actual details component later

**Part of**: reckon-4o2 (Phase 6: Task Scheduling & Calendar)
close_reason: Implemented smart positioning for task details pane based on selected task's section. Includes positioning logic, layout calculations, comprehensive tests, and all critical bug fixes from code review.

title: Add TUI keyboard shortcuts for task scheduling
description:
Implement keyboard shortcuts to schedule tasks and set deadlines from TUI.

## Keyboard Shortcuts

When task is focused in task list:

**'s' key** - Schedule task
- Opens date picker component
- User enters date (absolute or relative)
- Date saved to task's scheduled_date
- Task moves to appropriate section (TODAY/THIS WEEK/ALL)

**'D' key** - Set deadline (capital D to avoid conflict)
- Opens date picker component
- User enters deadline date
- Date saved to task's deadline_date
- Visual indicator updates

**'c' key** - Clear schedule/deadline
- Show submenu: "Clear: [S]chedule [D]eadline [B]oth ESC:cancel"
- 's' clears scheduled_date
- 'd' clears deadline_date
- 'b' clears both
- ESC cancels

## Integration

**File**: `internal/tui/model.go`

Handle key presses in task list:
```go
case "s":
    if m.focusedSection == SectionTasks && taskSelected {
        m.showDatePicker(DatePickerModeSchedule)
    }

case "D":
    if m.focusedSection == SectionTasks && taskSelected {
        m.showDatePicker(DatePickerModeDeadline)
    }

case "c":
    if m.focusedSection == SectionTasks && taskSelected {
        m.showClearDateMenu()
    }
```

**Date Picker Callbacks**:
- OnConfirm: Save date to task, refresh view, show success message
- OnCancel: Close picker, return to task list

**Success Messages**:
```
✓ Task scheduled for Jan 15
✓ Deadline set to Jan 20
✓ Schedule cleared
```

## Status Bar Updates

**File**: `internal/tui/components/status_bar.go`

Update hints for Tasks section:
```
Tasks: ↑↓:nav  space:toggle  enter:expand  s:schedule  D:deadline  c:clear  d:delete
```

## View Refresh

After scheduling:
- Task list re-renders
- Task moves to correct section (if grouping changed)
- Cursor follows task to new position
- Success message shown briefly

## Acceptance Criteria

- 's' key opens date picker for scheduling
- 'D' key opens date picker for deadline
- 'c' key opens clear menu with options
- Date picker integration works correctly
- Tasks move to correct section after scheduling
- Success messages shown after actions
- Status bar shows correct hints
- Works with both keyboard and no mouse needed

## Dependencies

- Requires: reckon-680 (Date picker component)
- Requires: reckon-mvl (Task model with schedule methods)
- Requires: reckon-3wq (Time-grouped view for section movement)

**Part of**: reckon-4o2 (Phase 6: Task Scheduling & Calendar)

title: Add visual indicators for task dates and status
description:
Display scheduled dates, deadlines, and status indicators in task rows.

## Visual Elements

### Date Display

Show date information in task rows:

• Review PR #123              📅 today

- 🟡 Yellow circle - Due soon (deadline within 2 days)

**With deadline only**:

• Task name  due Jan 15 🟡

**With both**:

func renderTaskRow(task Task, selected bool) string {
    // Build: checkbox + text + date info + status icon
}

    // Return icon based on deadline status

## Date Display Rules

- Show relative dates for recent items (today, tomorrow, yesterday)
- Show short date for current year (Jan 15)
- Show full date for other years (Jan 15, 2026)
- Align dates to right side of task list

## Acceptance Criteria

- Scheduled date displayed with 📅 icon
- Deadline date displayed with "due" prefix
- Overdue tasks show 🔴 icon and "overdue" text
- Due soon tasks show 🟡 icon

title: Implement time-grouped task list view (TODAY/THIS WEEK/ALL TASKS)
description:
Refactor task list to organize tasks by temporal proximity.

Replace flat task list with three time-based sections:
1. **TODAY** - scheduled/due today, overdue tasks

### Grouping Logic

**TODAY**:
- scheduled_date = today
- deadline_date = today
- deadline_date < today (overdue)

**THIS WEEK**:
- scheduled_date in current week (excluding today)
- deadline_date in current week (excluding today)
- Week = Monday-Sunday (or configurable)

**ALL TASKS**:
- scheduled_date > this week OR NULL
- deadline_date > this week OR NULL
- Tasks with status = someday

## Acceptance Criteria

- Task list shows three distinct sections
- Tasks automatically grouped based on scheduled/deadline dates
- Section headers show accurate counts
- Navigation works seamlessly across sections
- Cursor position preserved when tasks move between sections
- Empty sections show helpful message ("No tasks for today")
- Unit tests for grouping logic

## Dependencies

- Requires: reckon-mvl (Task model with dates)

**Part of**: reckon-4o2 (Phase 6: Task Scheduling & Calendar)

title: Implement CLI commands for task scheduling
description:
Add CLI commands for scheduling tasks and setting deadlines.

## Commands

**File**: `internal/cli/task.go`

Add subcommands to `rk task`:

### Schedule Command
```bash
rk task schedule <task-id> <date>     # Set scheduled date
rk task schedule <task-id> tomorrow   # Relative date
rk task schedule <task-id> mon        # Next Monday
rk task schedule <task-id> +3d        # 3 days from now
rk task schedule <task-id> --clear    # Remove schedule
```

### Deadline Command
```bash
rk task deadline <task-id> <date>     # Set deadline
rk task deadline <task-id> +7d        # Relative deadline
rk task deadline <task-id> --clear    # Remove deadline
```

### List Filters
Extend `rk task list`:
```bash
rk task list --scheduled=today        # Tasks scheduled for today
rk task list --scheduled=this-week    # Tasks scheduled this week
rk task list --scheduled=2025-01-15   # Tasks scheduled for specific date

rk task list --deadline=today         # Tasks due today
rk task list --deadline=this-week     # Tasks due this week
rk task list --overdue                # Past deadline

rk task list --grouped                # Show time-grouped output
```

## Date Parsing

Reuse date parser from date picker component:
- Absolute dates: YYYY-MM-DD
- Relative: today, tomorrow, mon-sun, +Nd, +Nw

## Output Format

When showing tasks with dates:
```
abc123  Fix critical bug                      📅 today  🔴 overdue
def456  Review PR #123                        📅 Jan 15
ghi789  Write documentation        due Jan 17 🟡
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- `rk task schedule <id> <date>` sets scheduled date
- `rk task deadline <id> <date>` sets deadline date
- Both commands support relative dates
- `--clear` flag removes date
- List filters work correctly (--scheduled, --deadline, --overdue)
- --grouped shows time-grouped output (TODAY, THIS WEEK, ALL TASKS)
- Error messages for invalid task IDs or dates
- Success messages confirm changes

## Dependencies

- Requires: reckon-mvl (Task model)
- Requires: reckon-9gt (Parser/writer)
- Can share date parser with: reckon-680 (Date picker)

**Part of**: reckon-4o2 (Phase 6: Task Scheduling & Calendar)

title: Create date picker component for TUI
description:
Build a reusable date picker component for scheduling tasks.

## Component

**File**: `internal/tui/components/date_picker.go`

**Features**:
- Text input for date entry
- Support absolute dates: YYYY-MM-DD, 2025-01-15
- Support relative dates:
  - `t` or `today` - today
  - `tm` or `tomorrow` - tomorrow
  - `mon`, `tue`, `wed`, `thu`, `fri`, `sat`, `sun` - next occurrence of weekday
  - `+3d` - 3 days from now
  - `+2w` - 2 weeks from now
- Validation with error messages
- ESC to cancel, Enter to confirm
- Show preview of parsed date

**UI**:
```
┌─ Schedule Task ──────────────┐
│ Date: +3d                    │
│ → 2025-01-14 (3 days)        │
│                              │
│ ESC: cancel  ENTER: confirm  │
└──────────────────────────────┘
```

## Date Parsing

**File**: `internal/tui/components/date_parser.go`

Helper functions:
```go
func ParseRelativeDate(input string) (time.Time, error)
func FormatDate(t time.Time) string  // YYYY-MM-DD
func NextWeekday(weekday string) (time.Time, error)
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Date picker component can be shown/hidden
- Supports absolute date input (YYYY-MM-DD)
- Supports relative shortcuts (t, tm, mon-sun, +Nd, +Nw)
- Shows preview of parsed date
- Validates input and shows errors
- Returns selected date or nil if cancelled
- Unit tests for date parsing logic

## Integration

Will be used by:
- Task scheduling ('s' key in task list)
- Task deadline setting ('d' key in task list)

## Dependencies

- None (standalone component)

**Part of**: reckon-4o2 (Phase 6: Task Scheduling & Calendar)

title: UX: Explore separate pane for task notes display
description:
Current state: Notes appear as indented items below tasks in the task list. For lengthy notes, this can clutter the task list and make it hard to scan.

Proposal: Display notes in a separate pane, potentially splitting the Tasks column vertically - tasks on top, selected task's notes below. This would:
- Keep task list clean and scannable
- Give notes more room for longer content
- Make it clearer which notes belong to which task
- Allow for better note formatting/wrapping

Consider: How to handle the split when no task is selected, or selected task has no notes.

title: UX: Make pane focus more visually obvious in TUI
description: Current state: Blue border indicates focus but is subtle. Initial focus is on Logs, not Tasks. Users may not realize they need to Tab to Tasks pane before Space/Enter work. Consider: brighter focus color, section title highlighting, or onboarding hint.

title: Add Log Expansion in TUI (Enter Key)

Solution: Add expansion logic similar to tasks: toggle collapsedMap, rebuild items, show notes under expanded entries; update help text. Files: log_view.go. Test: Enter on logs should show/hide notes like tasks.

title: Multi-day tasks: TUI cannot expand to show details, CLI list shows no details, task details from CLI not saved to markdown files
description: User reports that multi-day tasks cannot be expanded in the TUI to show details. The CLI task list command shows no details (only basic metadata). Task details entered via CLI (log entries) do not appear in the markdown files for the tasks. Investigation shows that the code has separate systems for journal tasks (expandable in TUI) and multi-day tasks (accessed via Ctrl+T picker). The CLI task show command outputs the markdown content, and logging should update the files, but user reports it doesn't work.

title: CLI task new: add interactive mode with title and description prompts
description:
## Problem

The CLI `rk task new` command is non-interactive and only accepts title as positional argument. Adding a description requires a separate `rk task edit <id> --description "..."` command, which is cumbersome.

Current usage:
```bash
rk task new "My task"           # Creates task, no description
rk task edit abc123 --description "Details here"  # Separate command needed
```

## Current Implementation

**File**: `internal/cli/task.go` lines 31-58

```go
var taskNewCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "new [title]",
    Short: "Create a new task",
    Args:  cobra.MinimumNArgs(1),
    RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
        title := strings.Join(args, " ")
        task, err := taskService.Create(title, taskNewTagsFlag)
        // ... outputs success message
    },
}
```

**Flags**: Only `--tags` supported at creation time

**Service**: `internal/task/service.go` - `Create()` accepts title and tags, not description

## Desired Behavior

Add an interactive mode when `rk task new` is run without arguments:

```bash
$ rk task new
Task title: Build user authentication
Description (optional, Enter to skip): 
> Implement JWT-based auth with refresh tokens.
> Include password reset flow.
> (blank line to finish)

✓ Created task: abc123def456
  Title: Build user authentication
```

Features:
1. **Title prompt**: Single-line input (required)
2. **Description prompt**: Multi-line input (optional, blank line or Ctrl-D to finish)
3. **Same inline TUI style** as proposed for `rk log` (reckon-8kq) - compact, doesn't clear terminal

## Implementation Options

### Option 1: Charmbracelet Huh Forms
Use the `huh` library for inline forms:
```go
form := huh.NewForm(
    huh.NewGroup(
        huh.NewInput().Title("Task title").Value(&title),
        huh.NewText().Title("Description (optional)").Value(&description),
    ),
)
```

### Option 2: Bubble Tea Mini-Program
Similar approach to reckon-8kq (log inline TUI):
- Small Bubble Tea program that doesn't clear terminal
- Title input followed by multi-line description input
- Enter on empty line finishes description

### Option 3: Simple Stdin Prompts
Basic `fmt.Print`/`bufio.Scanner` approach:
- Less polished but simpler
- Could work as MVP

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `rk task new` (no args) enters interactive mode
- [ ] Title prompt shown and required
- [ ] Description prompt shown and optional
- [ ] Multi-line description supported (blank line or Ctrl-D to finish)
- [ ] Task created with both title and description
- [ ] `rk task new "title"` still works (non-interactive, no description)
- [ ] `rk task new "title" --tags "a,b"` still works
- [ ] Terminal content above prompt remains visible (inline, not fullscreen)

## Files to Modify

- `internal/cli/task.go`:
  - Modify `taskNewCmd` to detect no-args case
  - Add interactive prompt logic
  - Call service with title and description

- `internal/task/service.go`:
  - Modify `Create()` to accept optional description parameter
  - OR add new `CreateWithDescription()` method

- Potentially create shared inline-prompt component if implementing for both this and reckon-8kq

## Related Tickets

- **reckon-8kq**: Improve CLI log entry UX with inline mini-TUI (same UX pattern)
- **reckon-z08**: Make task details easier to view and add (related but different scope)

Consider implementing a shared inline-prompt component that both tickets can use.

## Testing

- Test interactive mode creates task with title only
- Test interactive mode creates task with title and description
- Test multi-line description input
- Test empty description (just Enter)
- Test Ctrl-D/Ctrl-C cancellation
- Test non-interactive mode still works
- Test with --tags flag in non-interactive mode

title: Enable schedule add/delete in CLI and TUI
description:
## Problem

Users cannot add or delete schedule items despite the feature being fully implemented in the backend.

1. **CLI**: Commands exist in `internal/cli/schedule.go` but are NOT registered in root.go
2. **TUI**: Schedule section is read-only - can view items but cannot add/edit/delete

## Current Implementation

### Backend (Complete)
- **Model**: `ScheduleItem` struct with ID, Time, Content, Position
- **Service**: Full CRUD via `AddScheduleItem()`, `DeleteScheduleItem()`, `GetScheduleItems()`
- **Database**: SQLite table `schedule_items` with proper schema
- **Tests**: Comprehensive test coverage in `internal/journal/schedule_test.go`

### CLI (Implemented but Disconnected)
**File**: `internal/cli/schedule.go`

Commands that exist but aren't accessible:
- `rk schedule add [time] <content>` - Add item (time optional, HH:MM format)
- `rk schedule list` - Display today's schedule
- `rk schedule delete <index>` - Remove item by index

**Why disconnected**: `GetScheduleCommand()` is not called in `internal/cli/root.go`

### TUI (Display Only)
**File**: `internal/tui/components/schedule_view.go`

- ScheduleView renders items in right sidebar
- Items sorted by time (timed first, then untimed)
- Section navigable via Tab key
- NO keyboard handlers for add/edit/delete

## Desired Behavior

### CLI
1. Register schedule command in root.go
2. Users can run:
   - `rk schedule add 09:00 "Morning standup"`
   - `rk schedule add "Review PRs"` (no time)
   - `rk schedule list`
   - `rk schedule delete 1`

### TUI
1. When Schedule section is focused:
   - `a` or `s` key opens text entry for adding item
   - `d` key deletes selected item (with confirmation)
   - `Enter` could edit selected item (optional)
2. Update status bar hints for Schedule section

## Implementation Details

### CLI Fix (Simple)
**File**: `internal/cli/root.go` around line 52

Add:
```go
RootCmd.AddCommand(GetScheduleCommand())
```

### TUI Changes
**Files to modify**:

1. `internal/tui/model.go`:
   - Add `ModeSchedule` constant to entry modes
   - In key handler, when `SectionSchedule` focused and `a` pressed: activate text entry
   - Add delete handler for schedule items
   - Wire up submit callback for schedule mode

2. `internal/tui/components/text_entry_bar.go`:
   - Add `ModeSchedule` to mode enum if not present
   - Set prompt to "Add schedule item (HH:MM content): " or similar

3. `internal/tui/components/status_bar.go`:
   - Add hints for Schedule section: `a:add d:delete`

### Time Parsing
The service already handles time parsing:
- Accepts "HH:MM" or "HH:MM:SS" format
- Empty string for untimed items
- User could enter "09:00 Meeting" or just "Meeting"

## Acceptance Criteria

### CLI
- [ ] `rk schedule add` works with time and content
- [ ] `rk schedule add` works with content only (no time)
- [ ] `rk schedule list` shows today's items
- [ ] `rk schedule delete <index>` removes item

### TUI
- [ ] Can add schedule item when Schedule section focused
- [ ] Can delete schedule item when Schedule section focused
- [ ] Hints show available commands for Schedule section
- [ ] Items persist after restart

## Files to Modify

- `internal/cli/root.go` - Register GetScheduleCommand() (1 line)
- `internal/tui/model.go` - Add keyboard handlers and mode support
- `internal/tui/components/text_entry_bar.go` - Add ModeSchedule
- `internal/tui/components/status_bar.go` - Update hints

## Testing

- Test CLI add with time
- Test CLI add without time  
- Test CLI delete
- Test TUI add via keyboard
- Test TUI delete via keyboard
- Verify persistence after restart

title: CLI task list: use compact tabular format by default
description:
## Problem

The CLI `rk task list` output takes too much vertical space when there are more than 3-5 tasks. Each task currently consumes 4-5 lines in the default verbose format:

```
[1] [active] Task Title Here
  ID: abc123def456
  Created: 2026-01-04
  Tags: work, urgent

[2] [done] Another Task
  ID: xyz789uvw012
  Created: 2026-01-02
  Tags: personal
```

With 10 tasks, this is 40-50 lines of output - too much scrolling.

## Current Implementation

**File**: `internal/cli/task.go` lines 61-118

Three output modes exist:
1. **Default (verbose)**: 4-5 lines per task (ID, Created, Tags on separate lines)
2. **Compact** (`--compact` flag): Single line per task, but loses useful info
3. **JSON** (`--json` flag): Raw JSON array

The verbose output is rendered in `taskListCmd.RunE` (lines 105-114) using `fmt.Printf()`.

## Desired Behavior

Replace default output with a columnar/tabular format:

```
ID         STATUS   CREATED     TAGS          TITLE
abc123de   active   2026-01-04  work,urgent   Task Title Here
xyz789uv   done     2026-01-02  personal      Another Task
def456gh   active   2026-01-03  -             Third task with no tags
```

Features:
- Column headers for clarity
- Truncated ID (first 8 chars) to save space
- Aligned columns for readability
- Single line per task
- Tags joined with commas, or `-` if none
- Title can wrap or truncate based on terminal width

## Implementation Options

1. **Use `text/tabwriter`** from Go stdlib for auto-aligned columns
2. **Use `tablewriter`** library (github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter) for more features
3. **Manual formatting** with `fmt.Sprintf` and fixed widths

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Default output is tabular (no `--compact` flag needed)
- [ ] Column headers are shown
- [ ] One task per line
- [ ] All useful fields visible (ID, status, created, tags, title)
- [ ] Old verbose format available via `--verbose` or `-v` flag (optional)
- [ ] `--json` output unchanged
- [ ] Terminal width respected (truncate title if needed)

## Files to Modify

- `internal/cli/task.go`:
  - Lines 61-118: `taskListCmd` - change default rendering
  - Add `--verbose` flag if preserving old format
